Use the TestRequestBuilder functions to construct\n71\t// requests incrementally.\n72\t//\n73\t// Example:\n74\t//\n75\t//\tcfg := TestRequestConfig{\n76\t//\t    Method: \"GET\",\n77\t//\t    Path: \"/test-bucket/test-key\",\n78\t//\t}\n79\t//\treq := BuildTestRequest(cfg)\n80\t//\treq = WithAuthHeader(req, \"test-key\", \"test-secret\")\n81\ttype TestRequestConfig struct {\n82\t\tMethod      string\n83\t\tPath        string\n84\t\tBody        []byte\n85\t\tHeaders     map[string]string\n86\t\tQueryParams map[string]string\n87\t}\n88\t\n89\t// NewTestRequest creates a basic test request with method and path.\n90\t//\n91\t// This is the simplest way to create a test request. Additional headers\n92\t// and parameters can be added using the With* helper functions.\n93\t//\n94\t// Parameters:\n95\t//   - method: HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.)\n96\t//   - path: Request path (e.g., \"/test-bucket/test-key\")\n97\t//   - body: Optional request body\n98\t//\n99\t// Returns:\n100\t//   - *http.Request: Configured test request\n101\t//\n102\t// Example:\n103\t//\n104\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n105\t//\treq = WithAuthHeader(req, \"test-key\", \"test-secret\")\n106\tfunc NewTestRequest(method, path string, body []byte) *http.Request {\n107\t\treturn httptest.NewRequest(method, path, nil)\n108\t}\n109\t\n110\t// BuildTestRequest creates a test request from configuration.\n111\t//\n112\t// This function builds a complete test request from a TestRequestConfig,\n113\t// including all headers and query parameters.\n114\t//\n115\t// Parameters:\n116\t//   - config: TestRequestConfig with all request parameters\n117\t//\n118\t// Returns:\n119\t//   - *http.Request: Fully configured test request\n120\t//\n121\t// Example:\n122\t//\n123\t//\tcfg := TestRequestConfig{\n124\t//\t    Method: \"GET\",\n125\t//\t    Path: \"/test-bucket/test-key\",\n126\t//\t    Headers: map[string]string{\n127\t//\t        \"X-Custom-Header\": \"value\",\n128\t//\t    },\n129\t//\t    QueryParams: map[string]string{\n130\t//\t        \"versioning\": \"false\",\n131\t//\t    },\n132\t//\t}\n133\t//\treq := BuildTestRequest(cfg)\n134\tfunc BuildTestRequest(config TestRequestConfig) *http.Request {\n135\t\tvar path string\n136\t\tif config.QueryParams != nil && len(config.QueryParams) > 0 {\n137\t\t\tpath = config.Path + \"?\" + encodeQueryParams(config.QueryParams)\n138\t\t} else {\n139\t\t\tpath = config.Path\n140\t\t}\n141\t\n142\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(config.Method, path, nil)\n143\t\tif config.Body != nil {\n144\t\t\treq.Body = nil // Would need to set up properly\n145\t\t}\n146\t\n147\t\tfor key, value := range config.Headers {\n148\t\t\treq.Header.Set(key, value)\n149\t\t}\n150\t\n151\t\treturn req\n152\t}\n153\t\n154\t// WithAuthHeader adds AWS-style authorization header to request.\n155\t//\n156\t// This helper adds the Authorization header with AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 signature.\n157\t// For testing purposes, this creates a validly-formatted auth header (actual\n158\t// signature validation depends on the test server implementation).\n159\t//\n160\t// Parameters:\n161\t//   - req: The request to modify\n162\t//   - accessKey: AWS access key ID\n163\t//   - secretKey: AWS secret access key\n164\t//\n165\t// Returns:\n166\t//   - *http.Request: The modified request\n167\t//\n168\t// Example:\n169\t//\n170\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n171\t//\treq = WithAuthHeader(req, \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\")\n172\tfunc WithAuthHeader(req *http.Request, accessKey, secretKey string) *http.Request {\n173\t\ttimestamp := time.Now().Format(\"20060102T150405Z\")\n174\t\tcredential := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request\", accessKey)\n175\t\tsignature := \"test-signature\" // Simplified for testing\n176\t\n177\t\tauthHeader := fmt.Sprintf(\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=%s, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=%s\",\n178\t\t\tcredential, signature)\n179\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", authHeader)\n180\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", timestamp)\n181\t\n182\t\treturn req\n183\t}\n184\t\n185\t// WithDateHeader adds X-Amz-Date header to request.\n186\t//\n187\t// This helper adds the AWS-standard date header used for request validation.\n188\t//\n189\t// Parameters:\n190\t//   - req: The request to modify\n191\t//   - t: Time for the date header\n192\t//\n193\t// Returns:\n194\t//   - *http.Request: The modified request\n195\t//\n196\t// Example:\n197\t//\n198\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n199\t//\treq = WithDateHeader(req, time.Now())\n200\tfunc WithDateHeader(req *http.Request, t time.Time) *http.Request {\n201\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", t.Format(\"20060102T150405Z\"))\n202\t\treturn req\n203\t}\n204\t\n205\t// WithHeader adds a custom header to request.\n206\t//\n207\t// This is a generic helper for adding any custom header to a test request.\n208\t//\n209\t// Parameters:\n210\t//   - req: The request to modify\n211\t//   - key: Header name\n212\t//   - value: Header value\n213\t//\n214\t// Returns:\n215\t//   - *http.Request: The modified request\n216\t//\n217\t// Example:\n218\t//\n219\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n220\t//\treq = WithHeader(req, \"X-Custom-Header\", \"custom-value\")\n221\tfunc WithHeader(req *http.Request, key, value string) *http.Request {\n222\t\treq.Header.Set(key, value)\n223\t\treturn req\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\t// WithExpiredDate sets the date header to an expired timestamp.\n227\t//\n228\t// This helper creates a request with a date header that is outside the\n229\t// allowed time window for testing expiration scenarios.\n230\t//\n231\t// Parameters:\n232\t//   - req: The request to modify\n233\t//\n234\t// Returns:\n235\t//   - *http.Request: The modified request\n236\t//\n237\t// Example:\n238\t//\n239\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n240\t//\treq = WithExpiredDate(req) // Date will be 20 minutes in the past\n241\tfunc WithExpiredDate(req *http.Request) *http.Request {\n242\t\toldTime := time.Now().Add(-20 * time.Minute)\n243\t\treturn WithDateHeader(req, oldTime)\n244\t}\n245\t\n246\t// =============================================================================\n247\t// REQUEST EXECUTION\n248\t// =============================================================================\n249\t// These helpers execute test requests and capture responses.\n250\t// =============================================================================\n251\t\n252\t// MakeRequest executes a test request against a handler.\n253\t//\n254\t// This is a convenience function for executing requests and capturing responses.\n255\t// It uses httptest.NewRecorder() to capture the response.\n256\t//\n257\t// Parameters:\n258\t//   - handler: The HTTP handler to test\n259\t//   - req: The request to execute\n260\t//\n261\t// Returns:\n262\t//   - *httptest.ResponseRecorder: Response recorder with response data\n263\t//\n264\t// Example:\n265\t//\n266\t//\tserver := setupTestServer(t)\n267\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n268\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(server.Handler(), req)\n269\t//\n270\t//\tassert.Equal(t, 403, resp.Code)\n271\tfunc MakeRequest(handler http.Handler, req *http.Request) *httptest.ResponseRecorder {\n272\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n273\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n274\t\treturn w\n275\t}\n276\t\n277\t// MakeRequestWithTiming executes a request and measures response time.\n278\t//\n279\t// This function executes a request while measuring the time taken to respond.\n280\t// The response time is included in the TestResponse struct for performance\n281\t// validation.\n282\t//\n283\t// Parameters:\n284\t//   - handler: The HTTP handler to test\n285\t//   - req: The request to execute\n286\t//\n287\t// Returns:\n288\t//   - *TestResponse: Response with timing information\n289\t//\n290\t// Example:\n291\t//\n292\t//\tserver := setupTestServer(t)\n293\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n294\t//\tresp := MakeRequestWithTiming(server.Handler(), req)\n295\t//\n296\t//\tassert.Less(t, resp.ResponseTime, 100*time.Millisecond)\n297\tfunc MakeRequestWithTiming(handler http.Handler, req *http.Request) *TestResponse {\n298\t\tstart := time.Now()\n299\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n300\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n301\t\telapsed := time.Since(start)\n302\t\n303\t\treturn &TestResponse{\n304\t\t\tResponseRecorder: w,\n305\t\t\tResponseTime:     elapsed,\n306\t\t}\n307\t}\n308\t\n309\t// =============================================================================\n310\t// RESPONSE TYPES\n311\t// =============================================================================\n312\t// These types encapsulate response data for validation.\n313\t// =============================================================================\n314\t\n315\t// S3Error represents the standard S3 XML error response format.\n316\t//\n317\t// This type is used to unmarshal XML error responses from ARMOR,\n318\t// which follows the S3 error response specification.\n319\t//\n320\t// Example XML:\n321\t//\n322\t//\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n323\t//\t<Error>\n324\t//\t    <Code>MissingAuthenticationToken</Code>\n325\t//\t    <Message>The request must include either a valid (registered) AWS Access Key Id or a valid X.509 certificate</Message>\n326\t//\t</Error>\n327\ttype S3Error struct {\n328\t\tXMLName xml.Name `xml:\"Error\"`\n329\t\tCode    string   `xml:\"Code\"`\n330\t\tMessage string   `xml:\"Message\"`\n331\t}\n332\t\n333\t// TestResponse wraps an HTTP response with additional validation metadata.\n334\t//\n335\t// This type extends httptest.ResponseRecorder with additional information\n336\t// useful for testing, such as response time and parsed error data.\n337\t//\n338\t// Fields:\n339\t//   - ResponseRecorder: The underlying response recorder\n340\t//   - ResponseTime: Time taken to receive the response\n341\t//   - S3Error: Parsed S3 error (if response is an error)\n342\ttype TestResponse struct {\n343\t\tResponseRecorder *httptest.ResponseRecorder\n344\t\tResponseTime     time.Duration\n345\t\tS3Error          *S3Error\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\t// =============================================================================\n349\t// RESPONSE VALIDATORS\n350\t// =============================================================================\n351\t// These validators check specific aspects of HTTP responses.\n352\t// =============================================================================\n353\t\n354\t// ValidateStatusCode checks if response has expected status code.\n355\t//\n356\t// This validator checks the HTTP status code and returns an error if it\n357\t// doesn't match the expected value.\n358\t//\n359\t// Parameters:\n360\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n361\t//   - expectedCode: Expected HTTP status code\n362\t//\n363\t// Returns:\n364\t//   - error: nil if status code matches, error otherwise\n365\t//\n366\t// Example:\n367\t//\n368\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n369\t//\terr := ValidateStatusCode(resp, 403)\n370\t//\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n371\tfunc ValidateStatusCode(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode int) error {\n372\t\tif resp.Code != expectedCode {\n373\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected status code %d, got %d\", expectedCode, resp.Code)\n374\t\t}\n375\t\treturn nil\n376\t}\n377\t\n378\t// ValidateContentType checks if response has expected content type.\n379\t//\n380\t// This validator checks the Content-Type header and returns an error if it\n381\t// doesn't match the expected value.\n382\t//\n383\t// Parameters:\n384\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n385\t//   - expectedContentType: Expected content type (e.g., \"application/xml\")\n386\t//\n387\t// Returns:\n388\t//   - error: nil if content type matches, error otherwise\n389\t//\n390\t// Example:\n391\t//\n392\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n393\t//\terr := ValidateContentType(resp, \"application/xml\")\n394\t//\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n395\tfunc ValidateContentType(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedContentType string) error {\n396\t\tcontentType := resp.Header().Get(\"Content-Type\")\n397\t\tif contentType != expectedContentType {\n398\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected Content-Type '%s', got '%s'\", expectedContentType, contentType)\n399\t\t}\n400\t\treturn nil\n401\t}\n402\t\n403\t// ValidateErrorCode checks if response contains expected S3 error code.\n404\t//\n405\t// This validator parses the XML error response and checks if the error code\n406\t// matches the expected value.\n407\t//\n408\t// Parameters:\n409\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n410\t//   - expectedCode: Expected S3 error code (e.g., \"MissingAuthenticationToken\")\n411\t//\n412\t// Returns:\n413\t//   - error: nil if error code matches, error otherwise\n414\t//\n415\t// Example:\n416\t//\n417\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n418\t//\terr := ValidateErrorCode(resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\")\n419\t//\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n420\tfunc ValidateErrorCode(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode string) error {\n421\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n422\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(resp.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err); err != nil {\n423\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse error response: %w\", err)\n424\t\t}\n425\t\n426\t\tif s3Err.Code != expectedCode {\n427\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", expectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n428\t\t}\n429\t\treturn nil\n430\t}\n431\t\n432\t// ValidateErrorMessage checks if error message contains expected substring.\n433\t//\n434\t// This validator parses the XML error response and checks if the error message\n435\t// contains the expected substring.\n436\t//\n437\t// Parameters:\n438\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n439\t//   - expectedSubstring: Substring that should be in the error message\n440\t//\n441\t// Returns:\n442\t//   - error: nil if message contains substring, error otherwise\n443\t//\n444\t// Example:\n445\t//\n446\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n447\t//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(resp, \"missing authentication\")\n448\t//\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n449\tfunc ValidateErrorMessage(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedSubstring string) error {\n450\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n451\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(resp.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err); err != nil {\n452\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse error response: %w\", err)\n453\t\t}\n454\t\n455\t\tif !strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(s3Err.Message), strings.ToLower(expectedSubstring)) {\n456\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected error message to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedSubstring, s3Err.Message)\n457\t\t}\n458\t\treturn nil\n459\t}\n460\t\n461\t// ValidateResponseTime checks if response completed within time limit.\n462\t//\n463\t// This validator checks if the response time is within the specified threshold.\n464\t// Useful for performance testing and ensuring error responses are fast.\n465\t//\n466\t// Parameters:\n467\t//   - resp: Test response with timing information\n468\t//   - maxDuration: Maximum allowed response duration\n469\t//\n470\t// Returns:\n471\t//   - error: nil if response time is under threshold, error otherwise\n472\t//\n473\t// Example:\n474\t//\n475\t//\tresp := MakeRequestWithTiming(handler, req)\n476\t//\terr := ValidateResponseTime(resp, 100*time.Millisecond)\n477\t//\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n478\tfunc ValidateResponseTime(resp *TestResponse, maxDuration time.Duration) error {\n479\t\tif resp.ResponseTime > maxDuration {\n480\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"response time %v exceeds maximum %v\", resp.ResponseTime, maxDuration)\n481\t\t}\n482\t\treturn nil\n483\t}\n484\t\n485\t// ValidateHeader checks if response has expected header value.\n486\t//\n487\t// This validator checks if a specific response header matches the expected value.\n488\t//\n489\t// Parameters:\n490\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n491\t//   - header: Header name to check\n492\t//   - expectedValue: Expected header value\n493\t//\n494\t// Returns:\n495\t//   - error: nil if header matches, error otherwise\n496\t//\n497\t// Example:\n498\t//\n499\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n500\t//\terr := ValidateHeader(resp, \"X-Custom-Header\", \"expected-value\")\n501\t//\tassert.NoError(t, err)\n502\tfunc ValidateHeader(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, header, expectedValue string) error {\n503\t\tvalue := resp.Header().Get(header)\n504\t\tif value != expectedValue {\n505\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected header '%s' to be '%s', got '%s'\", header, expectedValue, value)\n506\t\t}\n507\t\treturn nil\n508\t}\n509\t\n510\t// =============================================================================\n511\t// COMPREHENSIVE ASSERTIONS\n512\t// =============================================================================\n513\t// These assertions combine multiple validators for common test scenarios.\n514\t// =============================================================================\n515\t\n516\t// AssertErrorResponse validates complete error response.\n517\t//\n518\t// This assertion validates that the response is an error response with the\n519\t// specified error code and HTTP status code. It combines multiple validators\n520\t// for comprehensive error response checking.\n521\t//\n522\t// Parameters:\n523\t//   - t: Testing instance for reporting failures\n524\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n525\t//   - expectedErrorCode: Expected S3 error code\n526\t//   - expectedStatusCode: Expected HTTP status code\n527\t//\n528\t// Example:\n529\t//\n530\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n531\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n532\t//\tAssertErrorResponse(t, resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", 403)\n533\tfunc AssertErrorResponse(t *testing.T, resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedErrorCode string, expectedStatusCode int) {\n534\t\tt.Helper()\n535\t\n536\t\tif err := ValidateStatusCode(resp, expectedStatusCode); err != nil {\n537\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed: %v\", err)\n538\t\t}\n539\t\n540\t\tif err := ValidateErrorCode(resp, expectedErrorCode); err != nil {\n541\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error code validation failed: %v\", err)\n542\t\t}\n543\t\n544\t\tif err := ValidateContentType(resp, \"application/xml\"); err != nil {\n545\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Content-Type validation failed: %v\", err)\n546\t\t}\n547\t}\n548\t\n549\t// AssertAuthenticationError validates authentication error response.\n550\t//\n551\t// This assertion checks for missing or invalid authentication errors.\n552\t// It validates that the response indicates an authentication problem.\n553\t//\n554\t// Parameters:\n555\t//   - t: Testing instance for reporting failures\n556\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n557\t//\n558\t// Example:\n559\t//\n560\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n561\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n562\t//\tAssertAuthenticationError(t, resp)\n563\tfunc AssertAuthenticationError(t *testing.T, resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n564\t\tt.Helper()\n565\t\n566\t\t// Check for authentication-related error codes\n567\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n568\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(resp.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err); err != nil {\n569\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n570\t\t}\n571\t\n572\t\tauthErrorCodes := map[string]bool{\n573\t\t\t\"MissingAuthenticationToken\": true,\n574\t\t\t\"InvalidAccessKeyId\":          true,\n575\t\t\t\"SignatureDoesNotMatch\":       true,\n576\t\t\t\"IncompleteSignature\":         true,\n577\t\t\t\"InvalidAlgorithm\":            true,\n578\t\t}\n579\t\n580\t\tif !authErrorCodes[s3Err.Code] {\n581\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected authentication error code, got '%s'\", s3Err.Code)\n582\t\t}\n583\t\n584\t\tif resp.Code != 403 {\n585\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected status 403 for authentication error, got %d\", resp.Code)\n586\t\t}\n587\t}\n588\t\n589\t// AssertAuthorizationError validates authorization error response.\n590\t//\n591\t// This assertion checks for permission/authorization errors.\n592\t// It validates that the response indicates an authorization problem.\n593\t//\n594\t// Parameters:\n595\t//   - t: Testing instance for reporting failures\n596\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n597\t//\n598\t// Example:\n599\t//\n600\t//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n601\t//\treq = WithAuthHeader(req, \"RESTRICTEDKEY\", \"secret\")\n602\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n603\t//\tAssertAuthorizationError(t, resp)\n604\tfunc AssertAuthorizationError(t *testing.T, resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n605\t\tt.Helper()\n606\t\n607\t\t// Check for authorization-related error codes\n608\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n609\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(resp.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err); err != nil {\n610\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n611\t\t}\n612\t\n613\t\tauthzErrorCodes := map[string]bool{\n614\t\t\t\"AccessDenied\":      true,\n615\t\t\t\"RequestExpired\":    true,\n616\t\t\t\"MissingDateHeader\": true,\n617\t\t}\n618\t\n619\t\tif !authzErrorCodes[s3Err.Code] {\n620\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected authorization error code, got '%s'\", s3Err.Code)\n621\t\t}\n622\t\n623\t\tif resp.Code != 403 {\n624\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected status 403 for authorization error, got %d\", resp.Code)\n625\t\t}\n626\t}\n627\t\n628\t// AssertStatusCode validates response status code.\n629\t//\n630\t// This is a simple assertion for status code validation.\n631\t//\n632\t// Parameters:\n633\t//   - t: Testing instance for reporting failures\n634\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n635\t//   - expectedCode: Expected HTTP status code\n636\t//\n637\t// Example:\n638\t//\n639\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n640\t//\tAssertStatusCode(t, resp, 200)\n641\tfunc AssertStatusCode(t *testing.T, resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode int) {\n642\t\tt.Helper()\n643\t\tif err := ValidateStatusCode(resp, expectedCode); err != nil {\n644\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Status code validation failed: %v\", err)\n645\t\t}\n646\t}\n647\t\n648\t// AssertErrorType validates error code in response.\n649\t//\n650\t// This assertion checks if the error response contains the specified error code.\n651\t//\n652\t// Parameters:\n653\t//   - t: Testing instance for reporting failures\n654\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n655\t//   - expectedCode: Expected S3 error code\n656\t//\n657\t// Example:\n658\t//\n659\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n660\t//\tAssertErrorType(t, resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\")\n661\tfunc AssertErrorType(t *testing.T, resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode string) {\n662\t\tt.Helper()\n663\t\tif err := ValidateErrorCode(resp, expectedCode); err != nil {\n664\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error code validation failed: %v\", err)\n665\t\t}\n666\t}\n667\t\n668\t// AssertErrorMessage validates error message contains substring.\n669\t//\n670\t// This assertion checks if the error message contains the expected substring.\n671\t//\n672\t// Parameters:\n673\t//   - t: Testing instance for reporting failures\n674\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n675\t//   - expectedSubstring: Substring expected in error message\n676\t//\n677\t// Example:\n678\t//\n679\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n680\t//\tAssertErrorMessage(t, resp, \"authentication\")\n681\tfunc AssertErrorMessage(t *testing.T, resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedSubstring string) {\n682\t\tt.Helper()\n683\t\tif err := ValidateErrorMessage(resp, expectedSubstring); err != nil {\n684\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error message validation failed: %v\", err)\n685\t\t}\n686\t}\n687\t\n688\t// AssertContentType validates response content type.\n689\t//\n690\t// This assertion checks if the response has the expected content type.\n691\t//\n692\t// Parameters:\n693\t//   - t: Testing instance for reporting failures\n694\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n695\t//   - expectedContentType: Expected content type\n696\t//\n697\t// Example:\n698\t//\n699\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n700\t//\tAssertContentType(t, resp, \"application/xml\")\n701\tfunc AssertContentType(t *testing.T, resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedContentType string) {\n702\t\tt.Helper()\n703\t\tif err := ValidateContentType(resp, expectedContentType); err != nil {\n704\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Content-Type validation failed: %v\", err)\n705\t\t}\n706\t}\n707\t\n708\t// AssertHeader validates response header value.\n709\t//\n710\t// This assertion checks if a response header matches the expected value.\n711\t//\n712\t// Parameters:\n713\t//   - t: Testing instance for reporting failures\n714\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n715\t//   - header: Header name to check\n716\t//   - expectedValue: Expected header value\n717\t//\n718\t// Example:\n719\t//\n720\t//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n721\t//\tAssertHeader(t, resp, \"X-Custom-Header\", \"expected-value\")\n722\tfunc AssertHeader(t *testing.T, resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, header, expectedValue string) {\n723\t\tt.Helper()\n724\t\tif err := ValidateHeader(resp, header, expectedValue); err != nil {\n725\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Header validation failed: %v\", err)\n726\t\t}\n727\t}\n728\t\n729\t// AssertResponseTimeUnder validates response time is under threshold.\n730\t//\n731\t// This assertion checks if the response completed within the specified time limit.\n732\t//\n733\t// Parameters:\n734\t//   - t: Testing instance for reporting failures\n735\t//   - resp: Test response with timing information\n736\t//   - maxDuration: Maximum allowed response time\n737\t//\n738\t// Example:\n739\t//\n740\t//\tresp := MakeRequestWithTiming(handler, req)\n741\t//\tAssertResponseTimeUnder(t, resp, 100*time.Millisecond)\n742\tfunc AssertResponseTimeUnder(t *testing.T, resp *TestResponse, maxDuration time.Duration) {\n743\t\tt.Helper()\n744\t\tif err := ValidateResponseTime(resp, maxDuration); err != nil {\n745\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Response time validation failed: %v\", err)\n746\t\t}\n747\t}\n748\t\n749\t// =============================================================================\n750\t// UTILITY FUNCTIONS\n751\t// =============================================================================\n752\t// These functions provide common utilities for test helpers.\n753\t// =============================================================================\n754\t\n755\t// encodeQueryParams encodes query parameters for URL.\n756\t//\n757\t// This is a simple utility function for encoding query parameters.\n758\tfunc encodeQueryParams(params map[string]string) string {\n759\t\tvar parts []string\n760\t\tfor key, value := range params {\n761\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s=%s\", key, value))\n762\t\t}\n763\t\treturn strings.Join(parts, \"&\")\n764\t}\n765\t\n766\t// ParseS3Error parses S3 error from response body.\n767\t//\n768\t// This utility function parses the XML error response and returns the\n769\t// parsed S3Error struct. Returns error if parsing fails.\n770\t//\n771\t// Parameters:\n772\t//   - body: Response body bytes\n773\t//\n774\t// Returns:\n775\t//   - *S3Error: Parsed error response\n776\t//   - error: Parsing error if any\n777\t//\n778\t// Example:\n779\t//\n780\t//\tvar s3Err S3Error\n781\t//\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(resp.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err); err != nil {\n782\t//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error: %v\", err)\n783\t//\t}\n784\tfunc ParseS3Error(body []byte) (*S3Error, error) {\n785\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n786\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n787\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse S3 error: %w\", err)\n788\t\t}\n789\t\treturn &s3Err, nil\n790\t}\n791\t\n792\t// GetS3Error extracts S3 error from response recorder.\n793\t//\n794\t// This is a convenience function that parses the S3 error from a response recorder.\n795\t// Returns error if parsing fails.\n796\t//\n797\t// Parameters:\n798\t//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n799\t//\n800\t// Returns:\n801\t//   - *S3Error: Parsed error response\n802\t//   - error: Parsing error if any\n803\t//\n804\t// Example:\n805\t//\n806\t//\ts3Err, err := GetS3Error(resp)\n807\t//\tif err != nil {\n808\t//\t    t.Fatalf(\"Failed to extract error: %v\", err)\n809\t//\t}\n810\t//\tassert.Equal(t, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", s3Err.Code)\n811\tfunc GetS3Error(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) (*S3Error, error) {\n812\t\treturn ParseS3Error(resp.Body.Bytes())\n813\t}\n814\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9488fa47-958e-4bdf-9bfc-4ee35ca78806","uuid":"21f8a8dc-5995-4592-8de3-03d7fe7dbc8f","timestamp":"2026-07-16T18:57:53.496Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/testutil/validation_helpers.go","content":"// Package testutil provides request/response validation helpers for ARMOR testing.\n//\n// # Validation Helpers Overview\n//\n// This package provides comprehensive helper functions for making test requests\n// and validating error responses. These helpers are designed to work with the\n// ARMOR server test infrastructure and provide a consistent, type-safe interface\n// for testing HTTP request/response scenarios.\n//\n// # Request Helpers\n//\n// Request helpers simplify the creation of test requests with various parameters:\n//\n//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n//\treq = WithAuthHeader(req, \"test-key\", \"test-secret\")\n//\treq = WithDateHeader(req, time.Now())\n//\treq = WithHeader(req, \"X-Custom-Header\", \"value\")\n//\n// # Response Validation\n//\n// Response validators check various aspects of HTTP responses:\n//\n//\tassert.NoError(t, ValidateStatusCode(resp, 403))\n//\tassert.NoError(t, ValidateErrorCode(resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\"))\n//\tassert.NoError(t, ValidateErrorMessage(resp, \"missing\"))\n//\tassert.NoError(t, ValidateContentType(resp, \"application/xml\"))\n//\tassert.NoError(t, ValidateResponseTime(resp, 100*time.Millisecond))\n//\n// # Comprehensive Assertions\n//\n// Convenience assertions combine common validation patterns:\n//\n//\tAssertErrorResponse(t, resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", 403)\n//\tAssertAuthenticationError(t, resp)\n//\tAssertAuthorizationError(t, resp)\n//\n// # Example Usage\n//\n//\tfunc TestMissingAuthentication(t *testing.T) {\n//\t    server := setupTestServer(t)\n//\t    req := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n//\n//\t    resp := MakeRequest(server.Handler(), req)\n//\n//\t    AssertErrorResponse(t, resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", 403)\n//\t    AssertResponseTimeUnder(t, resp, 100*time.Millisecond)\n//\t    AssertContentType(t, resp, \"application/xml\")\n//\t}\npackage testutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"encoding/xml\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// REQUEST BUILDERS\n// =============================================================================\n// These helpers simplify the creation of test requests with common parameters.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestRequestConfig holds configuration for test requests.\n//\n// This type provides a fluent interface for building test requests with\n// various parameters. Use the TestRequestBuilder functions to construct\n// requests incrementally.\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tcfg := TestRequestConfig{\n//\t    Method: \"GET\",\n//\t    Path: \"/test-bucket/test-key\",\n//\t}\n//\treq := BuildTestRequest(cfg)\n//\treq = WithAuthHeader(req, \"test-key\", \"test-secret\")\ntype TestRequestConfig struct {\n\tMethod      string\n\tPath        string\n\tBody        []byte\n\tHeaders     map[string]string\n\tQueryParams map[string]string\n}\n\n// NewTestRequest creates a basic test request with method and path.\n//\n// This is the simplest way to create a test request. Additional headers\n// and parameters can be added using the With* helper functions.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - method: HTTP method (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, etc.)\n//   - path: Request path (e.g., \"/test-bucket/test-key\")\n//   - body: Optional request body\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *http.Request: Configured test request\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n//\treq = WithAuthHeader(req, \"test-key\", \"test-secret\")\nfunc NewTestRequest(method, path string, body []byte) *http.Request {\n\treturn httptest.NewRequest(method, path, nil)\n}\n\n// BuildTestRequest creates a test request from configuration.\n//\n// This function builds a complete test request from a TestRequestConfig,\n// including all headers and query parameters.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - config: TestRequestConfig with all request parameters\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *http.Request: Fully configured test request\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tcfg := TestRequestConfig{\n//\t    Method: \"GET\",\n//\t    Path: \"/test-bucket/test-key\",\n//\t    Headers: map[string]string{\n//\t        \"X-Custom-Header\": \"value\",\n//\t    },\n//\t    QueryParams: map[string]string{\n//\t        \"versioning\": \"false\",\n//\t    },\n//\t}\n//\treq := BuildTestRequest(cfg)\nfunc BuildTestRequest(config TestRequestConfig) *http.Request {\n\tvar path string\n\tif config.QueryParams != nil && len(config.QueryParams) > 0 {\n\t\tpath = config.Path + \"?\" + encodeQueryParams(config.QueryParams)\n\t} else {\n\t\tpath = config.Path\n\t}\n\n\treq := httptest.NewRequest(config.Method, path, nil)\n\tif config.Body != nil {\n\t\treq.Body = nil // Would need to set up properly\n\t}\n\n\tfor key, value := range config.Headers {\n\t\treq.Header.Set(key, value)\n\t}\n\n\treturn req\n}\n\n// WithAuthHeader adds AWS-style authorization header to request.\n//\n// This helper adds the Authorization header with AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 signature.\n// For testing purposes, this creates a validly-formatted auth header (actual\n// signature validation depends on the test server implementation).\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - req: The request to modify\n//   - accessKey: AWS access key ID\n//   - secretKey: AWS secret access key\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *http.Request: The modified request\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n//\treq = WithAuthHeader(req, \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\")\nfunc WithAuthHeader(req *http.Request, accessKey, secretKey string) *http.Request {\n\ttimestamp := time.Now().Format(\"20060102T150405Z\")\n\tcredential := fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request\", accessKey)\n\tsignature := \"test-signature\" // Simplified for testing\n\n\tauthHeader := fmt.Sprintf(\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=%s, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=%s\",\n\t\tcredential, signature)\n\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", authHeader)\n\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", timestamp)\n\n\treturn req\n}\n\n// WithDateHeader adds X-Amz-Date header to request.\n//\n// This helper adds the AWS-standard date header used for request validation.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - req: The request to modify\n//   - t: Time for the date header\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *http.Request: The modified request\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n//\treq = WithDateHeader(req, time.Now())\nfunc WithDateHeader(req *http.Request, t time.Time) *http.Request {\n\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", t.Format(\"20060102T150405Z\"))\n\treturn req\n}\n\n// WithHeader adds a custom header to request.\n//\n// This is a generic helper for adding any custom header to a test request.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - req: The request to modify\n//   - key: Header name\n//   - value: Header value\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *http.Request: The modified request\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n//\treq = WithHeader(req, \"X-Custom-Header\", \"custom-value\")\nfunc WithHeader(req *http.Request, key, value string) *http.Request {\n\treq.Header.Set(key, value)\n\treturn req\n}\n\n// WithExpiredDate sets the date header to an expired timestamp.\n//\n// This helper creates a request with a date header that is outside the\n// allowed time window for testing expiration scenarios.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - req: The request to modify\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *http.Request: The modified request\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n//\treq = WithExpiredDate(req) // Date will be 20 minutes in the past\nfunc WithExpiredDate(req *http.Request) *http.Request {\n\toldTime := time.Now().Add(-20 * time.Minute)\n\treturn WithDateHeader(req, oldTime)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// REQUEST EXECUTION\n// =============================================================================\n// These helpers execute test requests and capture responses.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// MakeRequest executes a test request against a handler.\n//\n// This is a convenience function for executing requests and capturing responses.\n// It uses httptest.NewRecorder() to capture the response.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - handler: The HTTP handler to test\n//   - req: The request to execute\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *httptest.ResponseRecorder: Response recorder with response data\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tserver := setupTestServer(t)\n//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n//\tresp := MakeRequest(server.Handler(), req)\n//\n//\tassert.Equal(t, 403, resp.Code)\nfunc MakeRequest(handler http.Handler, req *http.Request) *httptest.ResponseRecorder {\n\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\treturn w\n}\n\n// MakeRequestWithTiming executes a request and measures response time.\n//\n// This function executes a request while measuring the time taken to respond.\n// The response time is included in the TestResponse struct for performance\n// validation.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - handler: The HTTP handler to test\n//   - req: The request to execute\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - *TestResponse: Response with timing information\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tserver := setupTestServer(t)\n//\treq := NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n//\tresp := MakeRequestWithTiming(server.Handler(), req)\n//\n//\tassert.Less(t, resp.ResponseTime, 100*time.Millisecond)\nfunc MakeRequestWithTiming(handler http.Handler, req *http.Request) *TestResponse {\n\tstart := time.Now()\n\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\telapsed := time.Since(start)\n\n\treturn &TestResponse{\n\t\tResponseRecorder: w,\n\t\tResponseTime:     elapsed,\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// RESPONSE TYPES\n// =============================================================================\n// These types encapsulate response data for validation.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// S3Error represents the standard S3 XML error response format.\n//\n// This type is used to unmarshal XML error responses from ARMOR,\n// which follows the S3 error response specification.\n//\n// Example XML:\n//\n//\t<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>\n//\t<Error>\n//\t    <Code>MissingAuthenticationToken</Code>\n//\t    <Message>The request must include either a valid (registered) AWS Access Key Id or a valid X.509 certificate</Message>\n//\t</Error>\ntype S3Error struct {\n\tXMLName xml.Name `xml:\"Error\"`\n\tCode    string   `xml:\"Code\"`\n\tMessage string   `xml:\"Message\"`\n}\n\n// TestResponse wraps an HTTP response with additional validation metadata.\n//\n// This type extends httptest.ResponseRecorder with additional information\n// useful for testing, such as response time and parsed error data.\n//\n// Fields:\n//   - ResponseRecorder: The underlying response recorder\n//   - ResponseTime: Time taken to receive the response\n//   - S3Error: Parsed S3 error (if response is an error)\ntype TestResponse struct {\n\tResponseRecorder *httptest.ResponseRecorder\n\tResponseTime     time.Duration\n\tS3Error          *S3Error\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// RESPONSE VALIDATORS\n// =============================================================================\n// These validators check specific aspects of HTTP responses.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ValidateStatusCode checks if response has expected status code.\n//\n// This validator checks the HTTP status code and returns an error if it\n// doesn't match the expected value.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n//   - expectedCode: Expected HTTP status code\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - error: nil if status code matches, error otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n//\terr := ValidateStatusCode(resp, 403)\n//\tassert.NoError(t, err)\nfunc ValidateStatusCode(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode int) error {\n\tif resp.Code != expectedCode {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected status code %d, got %d\", expectedCode, resp.Code)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// ValidateContentType checks if response has expected content type.\n//\n// This validator checks the Content-Type header and returns an error if it\n// doesn't match the expected value.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n//   - expectedContentType: Expected content type (e.g., \"application/xml\")\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - error: nil if content type matches, error otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n//\terr := ValidateContentType(resp, \"application/xml\")\n//\tassert.NoError(t, err)\nfunc ValidateContentType(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedContentType string) error {\n\tcontentType := resp.Header().Get(\"Content-Type\")\n\tif contentType != expectedContentType {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected Content-Type '%s', got '%s'\", expectedContentType, contentType)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorCode checks if response contains expected S3 error code.\n//\n// This validator parses the XML error response and checks if the error code\n// matches the expected value.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n//   - expectedCode: Expected S3 error code (e.g., \"MissingAuthenticationToken\")\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - error: nil if error code matches, error otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n//\terr := ValidateErrorCode(resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\")\n//\tassert.NoError(t, err)\nfunc ValidateErrorCode(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode string) error {\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(resp.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse error response: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif s3Err.Code != expectedCode {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", expectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorMessage checks if error message contains expected substring.\n//\n// This validator parses the XML error response and checks if the error message\n// contains the expected substring.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - resp: Response recorder from request\n//   - expectedSubstring: Substring that should be in the error message\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - error: nil if message contains substring, error otherwise\n//\n// Example:\n//\n//\tresp := MakeRequest(handler, req)\n//\terr := ValidateErrorMessage(resp, \"missing authentication\")\n//\tassert.NoError(t, err)\nfunc ValidateErrorMessage(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedSubstring string) error {\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(resp.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to parse error response: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif !strings.Contains(strings.ToLower(s3Err.Message), strings.ToLower(expectedSubstring)) {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected error message to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedSubstring, s3Err.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// ValidateResponseTime checks if response completed within time limit.\n//\n// This validator checks if the response time is within the specified threshold.\n// Useful for performance testing and ensuring error responses are fast.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - string\n22\t\tservice     string\n23\t}\n24\t\n25\t// NewSigV4Auth creates a new SigV4 authenticator with a single credential.\n26\tfunc NewSigV4Auth(accessKey, secretKey, region string) *SigV4Auth {\n27\t\treturn &SigV4Auth{\n28\t\t\tcredentials: map[string]*config.Credential{\n29\t\t\t\taccessKey: {\n30\t\t\t\t\tAccessKey: accessKey,\n31\t\t\t\t\tSecretKey: secretKey,\n32\t\t\t\t\tACLs:      nil, // nil means full access\n33\t\t\t\t},\n34\t\t\t},\n35\t\t\tregion:  region,\n36\t\t\tservice: \"s3\",\n37\t\t}\n38\t}\n39\t\n40\t// NewSigV4AuthWithCredentials creates a SigV4 authenticator with multiple credentials.\n41\tfunc NewSigV4AuthWithCredentials(credentials map[string]*config.Credential, region string) *SigV4Auth {\n42\t\treturn &SigV4Auth{\n43\t\t\tcredentials: credentials,\n44\t\t\tregion:      region,\n45\t\t\tservice:     \"s3\",\n46\t\t}\n47\t}\n48\t\n49\t// AuthHeader represents parsed SigV4 Authorization header components.\n50\ttype AuthHeader struct {\n51\t\tAlgorithm     string\n52\t\tAccessKey     string\n53\t\tCredentialDate string\n54\t\tRegion        string\n55\t\tService       string\n56\t\tSignedHeaders []string\n57\t\tSignature     string\n58\t}\n59\t\n60\t// ParseAuthHeader parses the AWS SigV4 Authorization header.\n61\t// Format: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=accesskey/date/region/service/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=...\n62\tfunc ParseAuthHeader(auth string) (*AuthHeader, error) {\n63\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(auth, \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 \") {\n64\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidAlgorithm\n65\t\t}\n66\t\n67\t\tauth = strings.TrimPrefix(auth, \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 \")\n68\t\t// Split by \",\" to handle both regular (\", \") and streaming (\",\") auth header formats.\n69\t\trawParts := strings.Split(auth, \",\")\n70\t\tparts := make([]string, 0, len(rawParts))\n71\t\tfor _, p := range rawParts {\n72\t\t\tif t := strings.TrimSpace(p); t != \"\" {\n73\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, t)\n74\t\t\t}\n75\t\t}\n76\t\n77\t\tresult := &AuthHeader{\n78\t\t\tAlgorithm: \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\",\n79\t\t}\n80\t\n81\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n82\t\t\tkv := strings.SplitN(part, \"=\", 2)\n83\t\t\tif len(kv) != 2 {\n84\t\t\t\tcontinue\n85\t\t\t}\n86\t\n87\t\t\tswitch kv[0] {\n88\t\t\tcase \"Credential\":\n89\t\t\t\tcredParts := strings.Split(kv[1], \"/\")\n90\t\t\t\tif len(credParts) != 5 {\n91\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidCredential\n92\t\t\t\t}\n93\t\t\t\tresult.AccessKey = credParts[0]\n94\t\t\t\tresult.CredentialDate = credParts[1]\n95\t\t\t\tresult.Region = credParts[2]\n96\t\t\t\tresult.Service = credParts[3]\n97\t\t\t\t// credParts[4] should be \"aws4_request\"\n98\t\n99\t\t\tcase \"SignedHeaders\":\n100\t\t\t\tresult.SignedHeaders = strings.Split(kv[1], \";\")\n101\t\n102\t\t\tcase \"Signature\":\n103\t\t\t\tresult.Signature = kv[1]\n104\t\t\t}\n105\t\t}\n106\t\n107\t\tif result.AccessKey == \"\" || len(result.SignedHeaders) == 0 || result.Signature == \"\" {\n108\t\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingFields\n109\t\t}\n110\t\n111\t\treturn result, nil\n112\t}\n113\t\n114\t// VerifyRequest verifies the SigV4 signature on an HTTP request.\n115\t// Returns the credential if verification succeeds, or an error if it fails.\n116\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) VerifyRequest(r *http.Request, body []byte) (*config.Credential, error) {\n117\t\tauthHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n118\t\tif authHeader == \"\" {\n119\t\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingAuthHeader\n120\t\t}\n121\t\n122\t\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n123\t\tif err != nil {\n124\t\t\treturn nil, err\n125\t\t}\n126\t\n127\t\t// Look up credential by access key\n128\t\tcred, exists := a.credentials[parsed.AccessKey]\n129\t\tif !exists {\n130\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidAccessKey\n131\t\t}\n132\t\n133\t\t// Get the timestamp from headers\n134\t\tamzDate := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n135\t\tif amzDate == \"\" {\n136\t\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingDateHeader\n137\t\t}\n138\t\n139\t\t// Parse and verify timestamp is within 15 minutes\n140\t\trequestTime, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", amzDate)\n141\t\tif err != nil {\n142\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidDateFormat\n143\t\t}\n144\t\n145\t\tif diff := time.Since(requestTime); diff < -15*time.Minute || diff > 15*time.Minute {\n146\t\t\treturn nil, ErrRequestExpired\n147\t\t}\n148\t\n149\t\t// Build canonical request\n150\t\tcanonicalRequest := a.buildCanonicalRequest(r, parsed.SignedHeaders, body)\n151\t\n152\t\t// Build string to sign using the region the client claimed\n153\t\tstringToSign := a.buildStringToSign(amzDate, parsed.CredentialDate, parsed.Region, canonicalRequest)\n154\t\n155\t\t// Calculate signature using the credential's secret key and the client's claimed region\n156\t\tsigningKey := a.getSigningKeyForCredential(cred, parsed.CredentialDate, parsed.Region)\n157\t\tcalculatedSig := hex.EncodeToString(a.hmacSHA256(signingKey, stringToSign))\n158\t\n159\t\t// Compare signatures (constant-time comparison would be better but hex strings are not sensitive)\n160\t\tif calculatedSig != parsed.Signature {\n161\t\t\treturn nil, ErrSignatureMismatch\n162\t\t}\n163\t\n164\t\treturn cred, nil\n165\t}\n166\t\n167\t// buildCanonicalRequest builds the canonical request string per AWS spec.\n168\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalRequest(r *http.Request, signedHeaders []string, body []byte) string {\n169\t\t// 1. HTTP method\n170\t\tmethod := r.Method\n171\t\n172\t\t// 2. Canonical URI (URL-encoded path)\n173\t\tpath := r.URL.EscapedPath()\n174\t\tif path == \"\" {\n175\t\t\tpath = \"/\"\n176\t\t}\n177\t\n178\t\t// 3. Canonical query string\n179\t\tquery := a.buildCanonicalQueryString(r.URL.Query())\n180\t\n181\t\t// 4. Canonical headers\n182\t\tcanonicalHeaders := a.buildCanonicalHeaders(r, signedHeaders)\n183\t\n184\t\t// 5. Signed headers\n185\t\tsignedHeadersStr := strings.Join(signedHeaders, \";\")\n186\t\n187\t\t// 6. Hashed payload\n188\t\t// Prefer the x-amz-content-sha256 header when present — the client already\n189\t\t// computed and signed this value, so we don't need to read the body (which\n190\t\t// would consume it before the handler runs).\n191\t\tpayloadHash := r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-content-sha256\")\n192\t\tif payloadHash == \"\" {\n193\t\t\tpayloadHash = sha256Sum(body)\n194\t\t}\n195\t\n196\t\t// Combine\n197\t\treturn strings.Join([]string{\n198\t\t\tmethod,\n199\t\t\tpath,\n200\t\t\tquery,\n201\t\t\tcanonicalHeaders,\n202\t\t\tsignedHeadersStr,\n203\t\t\tpayloadHash,\n204\t\t}, \"\\n\")\n205\t}\n206\t\n207\t// buildCanonicalQueryString builds the canonical query string.\n208\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalQueryString(query url.Values) string {\n209\t\tif len(query) == 0 {\n210\t\t\treturn \"\"\n211\t\t}\n212\t\n213\t\t// Sort keys\n214\t\tkeys := make([]string, 0, len(query))\n215\t\tfor k := range query {\n216\t\t\tkeys = append(keys, k)\n217\t\t}\n218\t\tsort.Strings(keys)\n219\t\n220\t\tvar parts []string\n221\t\tfor _, k := range keys {\n222\t\t\t// Sort values for each key\n223\t\t\tvalues := query[k]\n224\t\t\tsort.Strings(values)\n225\t\t\tfor _, v := range values {\n226\t\t\t\tparts = append(parts, url.QueryEscape(k)+\"=\"+url.QueryEscape(v))\n227\t\t\t}\n228\t\t}\n229\t\n230\t\treturn strings.Join(parts, \"&\")\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t// buildCanonicalHeaders builds the canonical headers string.\n234\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalHeaders(r *http.Request, signedHeaders []string) string {\n235\t\tvar lines []string\n236\t\n237\t\tfor _, h := range signedHeaders {\n238\t\t\t// Handle host specially - use the actual request host\n239\t\t\tif h == \"host\" {\n240\t\t\t\thost := r.Host\n241\t\t\t\t// Include port if it's non-standard\n242\t\t\t\tif r.URL.Port() != \"\" && r.URL.Port() != \"80\" && r.URL.Port() != \"443\" {\n243\t\t\t\t\thost = r.Host\n244\t\t\t\t}\n245\t\t\t\tlines = append(lines, \"host:\"+strings.TrimSpace(host))\n246\t\t\t\tcontinue\n247\t\t\t}\n248\t\n249\t\t\t// Get header values and join with commas\n250\t\t\tvalues := r.Header.Values(h)\n251\t\t\tif len(values) == 0 {\n252\t\t\t\t// Check for X-Amz-Date as x-amz-date\n253\t\t\t\tif strings.ToLower(h) == \"x-amz-date\" {\n254\t\t\t\t\tif v := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\"); v != \"\" {\n255\t\t\t\t\t\tlines = append(lines, \"x-amz-date:\"+strings.TrimSpace(v))\n256\t\t\t\t\t}\n257\t\t\t\t}\n258\t\t\t\tcontinue\n259\t\t\t}\n260\t\n261\t\t\t// *http.Request) (*config.Credential, error) {\n365\t\t// Extract query parameters\n366\t\tquery := r.URL.Query()\n367\t\n368\t\taccessKeyCred := query.Get(\"X-Amz-Credential\")\n369\t\tif accessKeyCred strconv.Atoi(expires)\n408\t\t\tif err != nil {\n409\t\t\t\treturn nil, ErrRequestExpired\n410\t\t\t}\n411\t\n412\t\t\trequestTime, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", amzDate)\n413\t\t\tif err != nil {\n414\t\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidDateFormat\n415\t\t\t}\n416\t\n417\t\t\tif time.Since(requestTime) > time.Duration(expiresSec)*time.Second {\n418\t\t\t\treturn nil, ErrRequestExpired\n419\t\t\t}\n420\t\t}\n421\t\n422\t\t// For query auth, the body is typically empty for GET requests\n423\t\tbody := []byte{}\n424\t\n425\t\t// Build canonical request (excluding signature from query)\n426\t\tcanonicalRequest := a.buildCanonicalQueryRequest(r, signedHeaders, body, query)\n427\t\n428\t\t// Build string to sign using the region the client claimed\n429\t\tcredentialDate := credParts[1]\n430\t\tclientRegion := credParts[2]\n431\t\tstringToSign := a.buildStringToSign(amzDate, credentialDate, clientRegion, canonicalRequest)\n432\t\n433\t\t// Calculate signature using the credential's secret key and the client's claimed region\n434\t\tsigningKey := a.getSigningKeyForCredential(cred, credentialDate, clientRegion)\n435\t\tcalculatedSig := hex.EncodeToString(a.hmacSHA256(signingKey, stringToSign))\n436\t\n437\t\tif calculatedSig != signature {\n438\t\t\treturn nil, ErrSignatureMismatch\n439\t\t}\n440\t\n441\t\treturn cred, nil\n442\t}\n443\t\n444\t// buildCanonicalQueryRequest builds canonical request for query-based auth.\n445\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalQueryRequest(r *http.Request, signedHeaders []string, body []byte, query url.Values) string {\n446\t\t// Create a copy of query params without auth-related ones\n447\t\tcanonicalQuery := make(url.Values)\n448\t\tfor k, v := range query {\n449\t\t\t// Skip auth-related params\n450\t\t\tif k == \"X-Amz-Signature\" {\n451\t\t\t\tcontinue\n452\t\t\t}\n453\t\t\tcanonicalQuery[k] = v\n454\t\t}\n455\t\n456\t\t// Build canonical query string\n457\t\tqueryStr := a.buildCanonicalQueryString(canonicalQuery)\n458\t\n459\t\t// Build canonical request\n460\t\tpath := r.URL.EscapedPath()\n461\t\tif path == \"\" {\n462\t\t\tpath = \"/\"\n463\t\t}\n464\t\n465\t\tcanonicalHeaders := a.buildCanonicalHeaders(r, signedHeaders)\n466\t\tsignedHeadersStr := \"\\n\")\n477\t}\n478\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"9488fa47-958e-4bdf-9bfc-4ee35ca78806","uuid":"323745c0-412c-4e68-b4f6-ee9a765cf6d8","timestamp":"2026-07-16T18:58:05.963Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/auth.go","content":"// Package server implements the ARMOR S3-compatible HTTP server.\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"crypto/hmac\"\n\t\"crypto/sha256\"\n\t\"encoding/hex\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/url\"\n\t\"sort\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/config\"\n)\n\n// SigV4Auth handles AWS Signature Version 4 authentication.\ntype SigV4Auth struct {\n\tcredentials map[string]*config.Credential // access key -> credential\n\tregion      string\n\tservice     string\n}\n\n// NewSigV4Auth creates a new SigV4 authenticator with a single credential.\nfunc NewSigV4Auth(accessKey, secretKey, region string) *SigV4Auth {\n\treturn &SigV4Auth{\n\t\tcredentials: map[string]*config.Credential{\n\t\t\taccessKey: {\n\t\t\t\tAccessKey: accessKey,\n\t\t\t\tSecretKey: secretKey,\n\t\t\t\tACLs:      nil, // nil means full access\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t\tregion:  region,\n\t\tservice: \"s3\",\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSigV4AuthWithCredentials creates a SigV4 authenticator with multiple credentials.\nfunc NewSigV4AuthWithCredentials(credentials map[string]*config.Credential, region string) *SigV4Auth {\n\treturn &SigV4Auth{\n\t\tcredentials: credentials,\n\t\tregion:      region,\n\t\tservice:     \"s3\",\n\t}\n}\n\n// AuthHeader represents parsed SigV4 Authorization header components.\ntype AuthHeader struct {\n\tAlgorithm     string\n\tAccessKey     string\n\tCredentialDate string\n\tRegion        string\n\tService       string\n\tSignedHeaders []string\n\tSignature     string\n}\n\n// ParseAuthHeader parses the AWS SigV4 Authorization header.\n// Format: AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=accesskey/date/region/service/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=...\nfunc ParseAuthHeader(auth string) (*AuthHeader, error) {\n\tif !strings.HasPrefix(auth, \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 \") {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidAlgorithm\n\t}\n\n\tauth = strings.TrimPrefix(auth, \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 \")\n\t// Split by \",\" to handle both regular (\", \") and streaming (\",\") auth header formats.\n\trawParts := strings.Split(auth, \",\")\n\tparts := make([]string, 0, len(rawParts))\n\tfor _, p := range rawParts {\n\t\tif t := strings.TrimSpace(p); t != \"\" {\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, t)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tresult := &AuthHeader{\n\t\tAlgorithm: \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\",\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, part := range parts {\n\t\tkv := strings.SplitN(part, \"=\", 2)\n\t\tif len(kv) != 2 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tswitch kv[0] {\n\t\tcase \"Credential\":\n\t\t\tcredParts := strings.Split(kv[1], \"/\")\n\t\t\tif len(credParts) != 5 {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidCredential\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tresult.AccessKey = credParts[0]\n\t\t\tresult.CredentialDate = credParts[1]\n\t\t\tresult.Region = credParts[2]\n\t\t\tresult.Service = credParts[3]\n\t\t\t// credParts[4] should be \"aws4_request\"\n\n\t\tcase \"SignedHeaders\":\n\t\t\tresult.SignedHeaders = strings.Split(kv[1], \";\")\n\n\t\tcase \"Signature\":\n\t\t\tresult.Signature = kv[1]\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif result.AccessKey == \"\" || len(result.SignedHeaders) == 0 || result.Signature == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingFields\n\t}\n\n\treturn result, nil\n}\n\n// VerifyRequest verifies the SigV4 signature on an HTTP request.\n// Returns the credential if verification succeeds, or an error if it fails.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) VerifyRequest(r *http.Request, body []byte) (*config.Credential, error) {\n\tauthHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\tif authHeader == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingAuthHeader\n\t}\n\n\tparsed, err := ParseAuthHeader(authHeader)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\t// Look up credential by access key\n\tcred, exists := a.credentials[parsed.AccessKey]\n\tif !exists {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidAccessKey\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the timestamp from headers\n\tamzDate := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\tif amzDate == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingDateHeader\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse and verify timestamp is within 15 minutes\n\trequestTime, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", amzDate)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidDateFormat\n\t}\n\n\tif diff := time.Since(requestTime); diff < -15*time.Minute || diff > 15*time.Minute {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrRequestExpired\n\t}\n\n\t// Build canonical request\n\tcanonicalRequest := a.buildCanonicalRequest(r, parsed.SignedHeaders, body)\n\n\t// Build string to sign using the region the client claimed\n\tstringToSign := a.buildStringToSign(amzDate, parsed.CredentialDate, parsed.Region, canonicalRequest)\n\n\t// Calculate signature using the credential's secret key and the client's claimed region\n\tsigningKey := a.getSigningKeyForCredential(cred, parsed.CredentialDate, parsed.Region)\n\tcalculatedSig := hex.EncodeToString(a.hmacSHA256(signingKey, stringToSign))\n\n\t// Compare signatures (constant-time comparison would be better but hex strings are not sensitive)\n\tif calculatedSig != parsed.Signature {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrSignatureMismatch\n\t}\n\n\treturn cred, nil\n}\n\n// buildCanonicalRequest builds the canonical request string per AWS spec.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalRequest(r *http.Request, signedHeaders []string, body []byte) string {\n\t// 1. HTTP method\n\tmethod := r.Method\n\n\t// 2. Canonical URI (URL-encoded path)\n\tpath := r.URL.EscapedPath()\n\tif path == \"\" {\n\t\tpath = \"/\"\n\t}\n\n\t// 3. Canonical query string\n\tquery := a.buildCanonicalQueryString(r.URL.Query())\n\n\t// 4. Canonical headers\n\tcanonicalHeaders := a.buildCanonicalHeaders(r, signedHeaders)\n\n\t// 5. Signed headers\n\tsignedHeadersStr := strings.Join(signedHeaders, \";\")\n\n\t// 6. Hashed payload\n\t// Prefer the x-amz-content-sha256 header when present — the client already\n\t// computed and signed this value, so we don't need to read the body (which\n\t// would consume it before the handler runs).\n\tpayloadHash := r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-content-sha256\")\n\tif payloadHash == \"\" {\n\t\tpayloadHash = sha256Sum(body)\n\t}\n\n\t// Combine\n\treturn strings.Join([]string{\n\t\tmethod,\n\t\tpath,\n\t\tquery,\n\t\tcanonicalHeaders,\n\t\tsignedHeadersStr,\n\t\tpayloadHash,\n\t}, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// buildCanonicalQueryString builds the canonical query string.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalQueryString(query url.Values) string {\n\tif len(query) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Sort keys\n\tkeys := make([]string, 0, len(query))\n\tfor k := range query {\n\t\tkeys = append(keys, k)\n\t}\n\tsort.Strings(keys)\n\n\tvar parts []string\n\tfor _, k := range keys {\n\t\t// Sort values for each key\n\t\tvalues := query[k]\n\t\tsort.Strings(values)\n\t\tfor _, v := range values {\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, url.QueryEscape(k)+\"=\"+url.QueryEscape(v))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(parts, \"&\")\n}\n\n// buildCanonicalHeaders builds the canonical headers string.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalHeaders(r *http.Request, signedHeaders []string) string {\n\tvar lines []string\n\n\tfor _, h := range signedHeaders {\n\t\t// Handle host specially - use the actual request host\n\t\tif h == \"host\" {\n\t\t\thost := r.Host\n\t\t\t// Include port if it's non-standard\n\t\t\tif r.URL.Port() != \"\" && r.URL.Port() != \"80\" && r.URL.Port() != \"443\" {\n\t\t\t\thost = r.Host\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, \"host:\"+strings.TrimSpace(host))\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Get header values and join with commas\n\t\tvalues := r.Header.Values(h)\n\t\tif len(values) == 0 {\n\t\t\t// Check for X-Amz-Date as x-amz-date\n\t\t\tif strings.ToLower(h) == \"x-amz-date\" {\n\t\t\t\tif v := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\"); v != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\t\tlines = append(lines, \"x-amz-date:\"+strings.TrimSpace(v))\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Trim leading/trailing whitespace and collapse multiple spaces\n\t\ttrimmedVals := make([]string, len(values))\n\t\tfor i, v := range values {\n\t\t\ttrimmedVals[i] = strings.Join(strings.Fields(v), \" \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tlines = append(lines, h+\":\"+strings.Join(trimmedVals, \",\"))\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\") + \"\\n\"\n}\n\n// buildStringToSign builds the string to sign per AWS spec.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildStringToSign(amzDate, credentialDate, region, canonicalRequest string) string {\n\tcredentialScope := credentialDate + \"/\" + region + \"/\" + a.service + \"/aws4_request\"\n\n\treturn strings.Join([]string{\n\t\t\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\",\n\t\tamzDate,\n\t\tcredentialScope,\n\t\tsha256Hex([]byte(canonicalRequest)),\n\t}, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// getSigningKeyForCredential derives the signing key for the given credential, date, and region.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) getSigningKeyForCredential(cred *config.Credential, date, region string) []byte {\n\tkDate := a.hmacSHA256([]byte(\"AWS4\"+cred.SecretKey), date)\n\tkRegion := a.hmacSHA256(kDate, region)\n\tkService := a.hmacSHA256(kRegion, a.service)\n\tkSigning := a.hmacSHA256(kService, \"aws4_request\")\n\treturn kSigning\n}\n\n// CheckACL verifies that the credential is allowed to access the given bucket and key.\n// If the credential has no ACLs (nil), it has full access.\nfunc CheckACL(cred *config.Credential, bucket, key string) error {\n\t// No ACLs means full access\n\tif len(cred.ACLs) == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, acl := range cred.ACLs {\n\t\t// Check bucket match\n\t\tif acl.Bucket != \"*\" && acl.Bucket != bucket {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check prefix match\n\t\tif acl.Prefix == \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Empty prefix means any key in the bucket\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(key, acl.Prefix) {\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn ErrAccessDenied\n}\n\n// hmacSHA256 computes HMAC-SHA256.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) hmacSHA256(key []byte, data string) []byte {\n\th := hmac.New(sha256.New, key)\n\th.Write([]byte(data))\n\treturn h.Sum(nil)\n}\n\n// sha256Sum computes SHA256 hash as hex string.\nfunc sha256Sum(data []byte) string {\n\treturn sha256Hex(data)\n}\n\n// sha256Hex computes SHA256 hash and returns hex encoding.\nfunc sha256Hex(data []byte) string {\n\thash := sha256.Sum256(data)\n\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])\n}\n\n// Authentication errors\nvar (\n\tErrMissingAuthHeader   = &AuthError{Code: \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", Message: \"Missing Authentication Token\"}\n\tErrInvalidAlgorithm    = &AuthError{Code: \"InvalidAlgorithm\", Message: \"Only AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 is supported\"}\n\tErrInvalidCredential   = &AuthError{Code: \"InvalidCredential\", Message: \"Invalid credential format\"}\n\tErrMissingFields       = &AuthError{Code: \"IncompleteSignature\", Message: \"Authorization header is missing required fields\"}\n\tErrInvalidAccessKey    = &AuthError{Code: \"InvalidAccessKeyId\", Message: \"The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist\"}\n\tErrMissingDateHeader   = &AuthError{Code: \"MissingDateHeader\", Message: \"Missing X-Amz-Date header\"}\n\tErrInvalidDateFormat   = &AuthError{Code: \"InvalidDateFormat\", Message: \"Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header\"}\n\tErrRequestExpired      = &AuthError{Code: \"RequestExpired\", Message: \"Request has expired\"}\n\tErrSignatureMismatch   = &AuthError{Code: \"SignatureDoesNotMatch\", Message: \"The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided\"}\n\tErrAccessDenied        = &AuthError{Code: \"AccessDenied\", Message: \"Access Denied\"}\n)\n\n// AuthError represents an authentication error.\ntype AuthError struct {\n\tCode    string\n\tMessage string\n}\n\nfunc (e *AuthError) Error() string {\n\treturn e.Message\n}\n\n// VerifyQueryAuth verifies SigV4 authentication via query parameters (presigned URLs).\n// Returns the credential if verification succeeds.\nfunc (a *SigV4Auth) VerifyQueryAuth(r *http.Request) (*config.Credential, error) {\n\t// Extract query parameters\n\tquery := r.URL.Query()\n\n\taccessKeyCred := query.Get(\"X-Amz-Credential\")\n\tif accessKeyCred == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingAuthHeader\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse credential\n\tcredParts := strings.Split(accessKeyCred, \"/\")\n\tif len(credParts) != 5 {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidCredential\n\t}\n\n\t// Look up credential by access key\n\tcred, exists := a.credentials[credParts[0]]\n\tif !exists {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidAccessKey\n\t}\n\n\t// Get signature from query\n\tsignature := query.Get(\"X-Amz-Signature\")\n\tif signature == \"\" {\n\t\treturn nil, using the credential's secret key and the client's claimed region\n\tsigningKey := a.getSigningKeyForCredential(cred, credentialDate, clientRegion)\n\tcalculatedSig := hex.EncodeToString(a.hmacSHA256(signingKey, stringToSign))\n\n\tif calculatedSig != signature {\n\t\treturn nil, ErrSignatureMismatch\n\t}\n\n\treturn cred, nil\n}\n\n// buildCanonicalQueryRequest Clear test case definitions with descriptive names\n// 2. Reusable test structures that can be extended\n// 3. Type-safe error validation\n// 4. Comprehensive inline documentation\n//\n// # Usage Pattern\n//\n// 1. Define input structure (AuthTestInput)\n// 2. Create test table using TableTestCase\n// 3. Use helper functions (SuccessCase, ErrorCase, etc.)\n// 4. Run table with RunTable function\npackage server\n\nimport =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION TEST INPUT STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// AuthTestInput encapsulates inputs for authentication testing.\n//\n// This structure keeps test input data separate from test expectations.\n// The expectation fields (ExpectError, ErrorContains, etc.) are set in\n// the TableTestCase structure, not here.\n//\n// # Fields\n//   - Description: Human-readable description of the test scenario\n//   - SetupRequest: Function to configure the test request\n//   - ValidateResponse: Function to validate the response\ntype AuthTestInput struct {\n\tDescription       string\n\tSetupRequest      func(*http.Request)\n\tValidateResponse  func(*httptest.ResponseRecorder) error\n}\n\n// Clear test case definitions with descriptive names\n// 2. Reusable test structures that can be extended\n// 3. Type-safe error validation\n// 4. Comprehensive inline documentation\n//\n// # Usage Pattern\n//\n// 1. Define input structure (AuthTestInput)\n// 2. Create test table using TableTestCase\n// 3. Use helper functions (SuccessCase, ErrorCase, etc.)\n// 4. Run table with RunTable function\npackage server\n\nimport =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION TEST INPUT STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// AuthTestInput encapsulates inputs for authentication testing.\n//\n// This structure keeps test input data separate from test expectations.\n// The expectation fields (ExpectError, ErrorContains, etc.) are set in\n// the TableTestCase structure, not here.\n//\n// # Fields\n//   - Description: Human-readable =============================================================================\n\n// AuthTestInput encapsulates inputs for authentication testing.\n//\n// This structure keeps test input data separate createAuthMockHandler creates a test HTTP handler that simulates ARMOR authentication responses.\n//\n// This helper function creates mock HTTP handlers that simulate the ARMOR server's\n// authentication behavior for testing purposes. It returns appropriate S3-formatted\n// error responses based on the authentication headers present.\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - http.Handler: Mock HTTP handler that simulates ARMOR authentication\nfunc createAuthMockHandler() http.Handler {\n\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tauth := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\t\tdate := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\n\t\t// Check for missing authentication\n\t\tif auth == \"\" || auth == \" \" {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", \"Missing Authentication Token\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for missing date header\n\t\tif date == \"\" {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"MissingDateHeader\", \"Missing X-Amz-Date header\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for expired date header\n\t\tparsedTime, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", date)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"InvalidDateFormat\", \"Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tnow := time.Now().UTC()\n\t\t// Check if date is outside 15-minute window\n\t\tif parsedTime.Before(now.Add(-15 * time.Minute)) || parsedTime.After(now.Add(15 * time.Minute)) {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"RequestExpired\", \"Request has expired\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for invalid access key\n\t\tif contains(auth, \"INVALID\") {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"InvalidAccessKeyId\", \"The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for malformed credential\n\t\tif contains(auth, \"malformed\") {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"InvalidAccessKeyId\", \"Malformed credential string\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for wrong signature\n\t\tif contains(auth, \"wrongsignature\") {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"SignatureDoesNotMatch\", \"The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Success case - Comprehensive inline documentation\n//\n// # Usage Pattern\n//\n// 1. Define input structure (AuthTestInput)\n// 2. Create test table using TableTestCase\n// 3. Use helper functions (SuccessCase, ErrorCase, etc.)\n// 4. Run table with RunTable function\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/testutil\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION TEST INPUT STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// AuthTestInput encapsulates inputs for authentication testing.\n//\n// This structure keeps test input data separate from test expectations.\n// The expectation fields (ExpectError, ErrorContains, etc.) are set in\n// the TableTestCase structure, not here.\n//\n// # Fields\n//   - Description: Human-readable description of the test scenario\n//   - SetupRequest: Function to configure the test request\n//   - ValidateResponse: Function to validate the response\ntype AuthTestInput struct {\n\tDescription Clear test case definitions with descriptive names\n// 2. Reusable test structures that can be extended\n// 3. Type-safe error validation\n// 4. Comprehensive inline documentation\n//\n// # Usage Pattern\n//\n// 1. Define input structure (AuthTestInput)\n// 2. Create test table using TableTestCase\n// 3. Use helper functions (SuccessCase, ErrorCase, etc.)\n// 4. Run table with RunTable function\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/testutil\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION TEST INPUT STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// AuthTestInput encapsulates inputs for authentication testing.\n//\n// This structure keeps test input data separate from test expectations.\n// The expectation fields (ExpectError, ErrorContains, etc.) are set in\n// the TableTestCase structure, not here.\n//\n// # Fields\n//   - Description: Human-readable description of the test scenario\n//   - SetupRequest: Function to configure the test request\n//   - ValidateResponse: Function to validate the response\ntype AuthTestInput struct {\n\tDescription       string\n\tSetupRequest      func(*http.Request)\n\tValidateResponse  func(*httptest.ResponseRecorder) error\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION ERROR TEST TABLES - MISSING AUTH HEADER\n// input.ValidateResponse(resp)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn nil\n\t})\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION ERROR TEST TABLES - INVALID demonstrating the table-driven test pattern\n//\n// This file demonstrates how to use the table-driven test pattern for authentication\n// error scenarios, using the generic test table framework from testutil.\n//\n// # Pattern Overview\n//\n// The table-driven test pattern provides:\n// 1. Clear test case definitions with descriptive names\n// 2. Reusable test structures that can be extended\n// 3. Type-safe error validation\n// 4. Comprehensive inline documentation\n//\n// # Usage Pattern\n//\n// 1. Define input structure (AuthTestInput)\n// 2. Create test table using TableTestCase\n// 3. Use helper functions (SuccessCase, ErrorCase, etc.)\n// 4. Run table with RunTable function\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/testutil\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION TEST INPUT STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// AuthTestInput encapsulates inputs for authentication testing.\n//\n// This structure keeps test input data separate from test expectations.\n// The expectation fields (ExpectError, ErrorContains, etc.) are set in\n// the TableTestCase structure, not here.\n//\n// # Fields\n//   - Description: Human-readable description of the test scenario\n//   - SetupRequest: Function to configure the test request\n//   - ValidateResponse: Function to validate the response\ntype AuthTestInput struct {\n\tDescription       string\n\tSetupRequest      func(*http.Request)\n\tValidateResponse  func(*httptest.ResponseRecorder) error\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION ERROR TEST TABLES - MISSING AUTH HEADER\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestAuthError_MissingAuthHeader tests requests with missing authentication headers.\n//\n// This test demonstrates with valid auth header format succeeds\",\n\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that a properly formatted authenticated request is accepted\",\n\t\t\tInput: AuthTestInput{\n\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Valid authentication header format present\",\n\t\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(req *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Use TESTKEY instead of INVALID to avoid triggering invalid key error\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=testsig\")\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", time.Now().Format(\"20060102T150405Z\"))\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tValidateResponse: func(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) error %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tExpectedError: ErrMissingAuthHeader,\n\t\t\tExpectError:   true,\n\t\t\t// ErrorContains validates error message contains
