Clear test case definitions with descriptive names\n// 2. Reusable test structures that can be extended\n// 3. Type-safe error validation\n// 4. Comprehensive inline documentation\n//\n// # Usage Pattern\n//\n// 1. Define input structure (AuthTestInput)\n// 2. Create test table using TableTestCase\n// 3. Use helper functions (SuccessCase, ErrorCase, etc.)\n// 4. Run table with RunTable function\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/testutil\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION TEST INPUT STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// AuthTestInput encapsulates inputs for authentication testing.\n//\n// This structure keeps test input data separate from test expectations.\n// The expectation fields (ExpectError, ErrorContains, etc.) are set in\n// the TableTestCase structure, not here.\n//\n// # Fields\n//   - Description: Human-readable description of the test scenario\n//   - SetupRequest: Function to configure the test request\n//   - ValidateResponse: Function to validate the response\ntype AuthTestInput struct {\n\tDescription       string\n\tSetupRequest      func(*http.Request)\n\tValidateResponse  func(*httptest.ResponseRecorder) error\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION ERROR TEST TABLES - MISSING AUTH HEADER\n// createAuthMockHandler()\n\t\treq := testutil.NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\n\t\tif input.SetupRequest != nil {\n\t\t\tinput.SetupRequest(req)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresp := testutil.MakeRequest(handler, req)\n\n\t\tif input.ValidateResponse != nil {\n\t\t\treturn input.ValidateResponse(resp)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn nil\n\t})\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// MOCK HANDLERS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// createAuthMockHandler creates a test HTTP handler that simulates ARMOR authentication responses.\n//\n// This helper function creates mock HTTP handlers that simulate the ARMOR server's\n// authentication behavior for testing purposes. It returns appropriate S3-formatted\n// error responses based on the authentication headers present.\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - http.Handler: Mock HTTP handler that simulates ARMOR authentication\nfunc createAuthMockHandler() http.Handler {\n\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tauth := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\t\tdate := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\n\t\t// Check for missing authentication\n\t\tif auth == \"\" || auth == \" \" {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", \"Missing Authentication Token\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for missing date header\n\t\tif date == \"\" {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"MissingDateHeader\", \"Missing X-Amz-Date header\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for expired date header\n\t\tparsedTime, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", date)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"InvalidDateFormat\", \"Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tnow := time.Now().UTC()\n\t\t// Check if date is outside 15-minute window\n\t\tif parsedTime.Before(now.Add(-15 * time.Minute)) || parsedTime.After(now.Add(15 * time.Minute)) {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"RequestExpired\", \"Request has expired\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for invalid access key\n\t\tif contains(auth, \"INVALID\") {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"InvalidAccessKeyId\", \"The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for malformed credential\n\t\tif contains(auth, \"malformed\") {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"InvalidAccessKeyId\", \"Malformed credential string\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for wrong signature\n\t\tif contains(auth, \"wrongsignature\") {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"SignatureDoesNotMatch\", \"The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Success case - valid authentication\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"OK\"))\n\t})\n}\n\n// sendS3Error sends a properly formatted S3 error response.\n//\n// This helper function constructs S3-compatible XML error responses,\n// matching the format used by the real ARMOR server.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - Clear test case definitions with descriptive names\n// 2. Reusable test structures that can be extended\n// 3. Type-safe error validation\n// 4. Comprehensive inline documentation\n//\n// # Usage Pattern\n//\n// 1. Define input structure (AuthTestInput)\n// 2. Create test table using TableTestCase\n// 3. Use helper functions (SuccessCase, ErrorCase, etc.)\n// 4. Run table with RunTable function\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/testutil\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION TEST INPUT STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// AuthTestInput encapsulates inputs for authentication testing.\n//\n// This structure keeps test input data separate from test expectations.\n// The expectation fields (ExpectError, ErrorContains, etc.) are set in\n// the TableTestCase structure, not here.\n//\n// # Fields\n//   - Description: Human-readable description of the test scenario\n//   - SetupRequest: Function to configure the test request\n//   - ValidateResponse: Function to validate the response\ntype AuthTestInput struct {\n\tDescription       string\n\tSetupRequest      func(*http.Request)\n\tValidateResponse  func(*httptest.ResponseRecorder) error\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION ERROR TEST TABLES - MISSING AUTH HEADER\n// Clear test case definitions with descriptive names\n// 2. Reusable test structures that can be extended\n// 3. Type-safe error validation\n// 4. Comprehensive inline documentation\n//\n// # Usage Pattern\n//\n// 1. Define input structure (AuthTestInput)\n// 2. Create test table using TableTestCase\n// 3. Use helper functions (SuccessCase, ErrorCase, etc.)\n// 4. Run table with RunTable function\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/testutil\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION TEST INPUT STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// AuthTestInput encapsulates inputs for authentication testing.\n//\n// This structure keeps test input data separate from test expectations.\n// The expectation fields (ExpectError, ErrorContains, etc.) are set in\n// the TableTestCase structure, not here.\n//\n// # Fields\n//   - Description: Human-readable description of the test scenario\n//   - SetupRequest: Function to configure the test request\n//   - ValidateResponse: Function to validate the response\ntype AuthTestInput struct {\n\tDescription       string\n\tSetupRequest      func(*http.Request)\n\tValidateResponse  func(*httptest.ResponseRecorder) error\n}\n\n// testutil.\n//\n// # Pattern Overview\n//\n// The table-driven test pattern provides:\n// 1. Clear test case definitions with descriptive names\n// 2. Reusable test structures that can be extended\n// 3. Type-safe error validation\n// 4. Comprehensive inline documentation\n//\n// # Usage Pattern\n//\n// 1. Define input structure (AuthTestInput)\n// 2. Create test table using TableTestCase\n// 3. Use helper functions (SuccessCase, ErrorCase, etc.)\n// 4. Run table with RunTable function\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/testutil\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION TEST INPUT STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// AuthTestInput encapsulates inputs for authentication testing.\n//\n// This structure keeps test input data separate from test expectations.\n// The expectation fields (ExpectError, ErrorContains, etc.) are set in\n// the TableTestCase structure, not here.\n//\n// # Fields\n//   - Description: Human-readable description of the test scenario\n//   - SetupRequest: Function to configure the test request\n//   - ValidateResponse: Function to validate the response\ntype AuthTestInput struct {\n\tDescription table-driven test pattern provides:\n// 1. Clear test case definitions with descriptive names\n// 2. Reusable test structures that can be extended\n// 3. Type-safe error validation\n// 4. Comprehensive inline documentation\n//\n// # Usage Pattern\n//\n// 1. Define input structure (AuthTestInput)\n// 2. Create test table using TableTestCase\n// 3. Use helper functions (SuccessCase, ErrorCase, etc.)\n// 4. Run table with RunTable function\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/testutil\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION TEST INPUT STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// AuthTestInput encapsulates inputs for authentication testing.\n//\n// This structure keeps test input data separate from test expectations.\n// The expectation fields (ExpectError, ErrorContains, etc.) are set in\n// the TableTestCase structure, not here.\n//\n// # Fields\n//   - Description: Human-readable description of the test scenario\n//   - SetupRequest: Function to configure the test request\n//   - ValidateResponse: Function to validate the response\ntype AuthTestInput struct {\n\tDescription       string\n\tSetupRequest      func(*http.Request)\n\tValidateResponse  func(*httptest.ResponseRecorder) error\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION ERROR TEST TABLES - MISSING AUTH HEADER\n// authentication behavior for testing purposes. It returns appropriate S3-formatted\n// error responses based on the authentication headers present.\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - http.Handler: Mock HTTP handler that simulates ARMOR authentication\nfunc createAuthMockHandler() http.Handler {\n\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tauth := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\t\tdate := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\n\t\t// Check for missing authentication\n\t\tif auth == \"\" || auth == \" \" {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", \"Missing Authentication Token\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for missing date header\n\t\tif date == \"\" {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"MissingDateHeader\", \"Missing X-Amz-Date header\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for expired date header\n\t\tparsedTime, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", date)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"InvalidDateFormat\", \"Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tnow := time.Now().UTC()\n\t\t// Check if date is outside 15-minute window\n\t\tif parsedTime.Before(now.Add(-15 * time.Minute)) || parsedTime.After(now.Add(15 * time.Minute)) {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"RequestExpired\", \"Request has expired\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for invalid access key\n\t\tif contains(auth, \"INVALID\") {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"InvalidAccessKeyId\", \"The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for malformed credential\n\t\tif contains(auth, \"malformed\") {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"InvalidAccessKeyId\", \"Malformed credential string\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for wrong signature\n\t\tif contains(auth, \"wrongsignature\") {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"SignatureDoesNotMatch\", \"The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Success case - Clear test case definitions with descriptive names\n// 2. Reusable test structures that can be extended\n// 3. Type-safe error validation\n// 4. Comprehensive inline documentation\n//\n// # Usage Pattern\n//\n// 1. Define input structure (AuthTestInput)\n// 2. Create test table using TableTestCase\n// 3. Use helper functions (SuccessCase, ErrorCase, etc.)\n// 4. Run table with RunTable function\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/testutil\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION TEST INPUT STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// AuthTestInput encapsulates inputs for authentication testing.\n//\n// This structure keeps test input data separate from test expectations.\n// The expectation fields (ExpectError, ErrorContains, etc.) are set in\n// the TableTestCase structure, not here.\n//\n// # Fields\n//   - Description: Human-readable description of the test scenario\n//   - SetupRequest: Function to configure the test request\n//   - ValidateResponse: Function to validate the response\ntype AuthTestInput struct {\n\tDescription       string\n\tSetupRequest      func(*http.Request)\n\tValidateResponse  func(*httptest.ResponseRecorder) error\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION ERROR TEST TABLES - MISSING AUTH HEADER\n// =============================================================================\n// MOCK HANDLERS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// createAuthMockHandler creates a test HTTP handler that simulates ARMOR authentication responses.\n//\n// This helper function creates mock HTTP handlers that simulate the ARMOR server's\n// authentication behavior for testing purposes. It returns appropriate S3-formatted\n// error responses based on the authentication headers present.\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - http.Handler: Mock HTTP handler that simulates ARMOR authentication\nfunc createAuthMockHandler() http.Handler {\n\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tauth := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\t\tdate := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\n\t\t// Check for missing authentication\n\t\tif auth == \"\" || auth == \" \" {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", \"Missing Authentication Token\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for missing date header\n\t\tif date == \"\" {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"MissingDateHeader\", \"Missing X-Amz-Date header\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for expired date header\n\t\tparsedTime, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", date)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"InvalidDateFormat\", \"Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tnow := time.Now().UTC()\n\t\t// Check if date is outside 15-minute window\n\t\tif parsedTime.Before(now.Add(-15 * time.Minute)) || parsedTime.After(now.Add(15 * time.Minute)) {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"RequestExpired\", \"Request has expired\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for invalid access key\n\t\tif contains(auth, \"INVALID\") {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"InvalidAccessKeyId\", \"The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for malformed credential\n\t\tif contains(auth, \"malformed\") {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"InvalidAccessKeyId\", \"Malformed credential string\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for wrong signature\n\t\tif contains(auth, \"wrongsignature\") {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"SignatureDoesNotMatch\", \"The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Success case - valid authentication\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"OK\"))\n\t})\n}\n\n// sendS3Error sends a properly formatted S3 error response.\n//\n// This helper function constructs S3-compatible XML error responses,\n// matching the format used by the real ARMOR server.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - w: HTTP response writer\n//   - code: S3 error code (e.g., \"MissingAuthenticationToken\")\n//   - message: Human-readable error message\nfunc sendS3Error(w http.ResponseWriter, code, message string) {\n\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusForbidden)\n\txml := fmt.Sprintf(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?><Error><Code>%s</Code><Message>%s</Message></Error>`, code, message)\n\tw.Write([]byte(xml))\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":480,"oldLines":6,"newStart":480,"newLines":7,"lines":["           req.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", time.Now().Format(\"20060102T150405Z\"))","         },","         ValidateResponse: func(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) error {","+          // Valid authentication should return 200","           if err := testutil.ValidateStatusCode(resp, 200); err != nil {","             return fmt.Errorf(\"status validation failed: %w\", err)","           }"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

Clear test case definitions with descriptive names\n// 2. Reusable test structures that can be extended\n// 3. Type-safe error validation\n// 4. Comprehensive inline documentation\n//\n// # Usage Pattern\n//\n// 1. Define input structure (AuthTestInput)\n// 2. Create test table using TableTestCase\n// 3. Use helper functions (SuccessCase, ErrorCase, etc.)\n// 4. Run table with RunTable function\npackage server\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/jedarden/armor/internal/testutil\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION TEST INPUT STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// AuthTestInput encapsulates inputs for authentication testing.\n//\n// This structure keeps test input data separate from test expectations.\n// The expectation fields (ExpectError, ErrorContains, etc.) are set in\n// the TableTestCase structure, not here.\n//\n// # Fields\n//   - Description: Human-readable description of the test scenario\n//   - SetupRequest: Function to configure the test request\n//   - ValidateResponse: Function to validate the response\ntype AuthTestInput struct {\n\tDescription       string\n\tSetupRequest      func(*http.Request)\n\tValidateResponse  func(*httptest.ResponseRecorder) error\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION ERROR TEST TABLES - MISSING AUTH HEADER\n// with valid auth header format succeeds\",\n\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that a properly formatted authenticated request is accepted\",\n\t\t\tInput: AuthTestInput{\n\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Valid authentication header format present\",\n\t\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(req *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Use TESTKEY instead of INVALID to avoid triggering invalid key error\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, error\",\n\t\t\tInput: AuthTestInput{\n\t\t\t\tDescription: \"No Authorization header provided\",\n\t\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(req *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\t// No auth header 403 {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected status 403, got %d\", resp.Code)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate S3 error format using testutil helpers\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := message contains expected substring\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutil.ValidateErrorMessage(resp, \"Missing Authentication\"); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error message validation failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tExpectedError: ErrMissingAuthHeader,\n\t\t\tExpectError:   true,\n\t\t\t// ErrorContains validates error message contains this substring (case-insensitive)\n\t\t\tErrorContains: \"missing\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tName:        \"request with empty auth header fails\",\n\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that an empty =============================================================================\n\n// createAuthMockHandler creates a test HTTP handler that simulates ARMOR authentication responses.\n//\n// This helper function creates mock HTTP handlers that simulate the ARMOR server's\n// authentication behavior for testing purposes. It returns appropriate S3-formatted\n// error responses based on the authentication headers present.\n//\n// Returns:\n//   - http.Handler: Mock HTTP handler that simulates ARMOR authentication\nfunc createAuthMockHandler() http.Handler {\n\treturn http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n\t\tauth := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n\t\tdate := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\")\n\n\t\t// Check for missing authentication\n\t\tif auth == \"\" || auth == \" \" {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\", \"Missing Authentication Token\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for missing date header\n\t\tif date == \"\" {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"MissingDateHeader\", \"Missing X-Amz-Date header\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for expired date header\n\t\tparsedTime, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", date)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"InvalidDateFormat\", \"Invalid date format in X-Amz-Date header\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tnow := time.Now().UTC()\n\t\t// Check if date is outside 15-minute window\n\t\tif parsedTime.Before(now.Add(-15 * time.Minute)) || parsedTime.After(now.Add(15 * time.Minute)) {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"RequestExpired\", \"Request has expired\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for invalid access key\n\t\tif contains(auth, \"INVALID\") {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"InvalidAccessKeyId\", \"The AWS Access Key Id you provided does not exist in our records\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for malformed credential\n\t\tif contains(auth, \"malformed\") {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"InvalidAccessKeyId\", \"Malformed credential string\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check for wrong signature\n\t\tif contains(auth, \"wrongsignature\") {\n\t\t\tsendS3Error(w, \"SignatureDoesNotMatch\", \"The request signature we calculated does not match the signature you provided\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Success case - valid authentication\n\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n\t\tw.Write([]byte(\"OK\"))\n\t})\n}\n\n// sendS3Error sends a properly formatted S3 error response.\n//\n// This helper function constructs S3-compatible XML error responses,\n// matching the format used by the real ARMOR server.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION ERROR TEST TABLES - MISSING AUTH HEADER\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestAuthError_MissingAuthHeader tests requests with missing authentication headers.\n//\n// This test demonstrates the table-driven pattern for testing missing authentication\n// scenarios using the generic test table framework.\nfunc TestAuthError_MissingAuthHeader(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Define test cases using the table-driven pattern\n\t// Each case uses TableTestCase to define inputs and expectations clearly\n\ttable := []testutil.TableTestCase[AuthTestInput, error]{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tName:        \"request with valid auth header format succeeds\",\n\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that a properly formatted authenticated request is accepted\",\n\t\t\tInput: AuthTestInput{\n\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Valid authentication header format present\",\n\t\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(req *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Use TESTKEY instead of INVALID to avoid triggering invalid key error\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=testsig\")\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", time.Now().Format(\"20060102T150405Z\"))\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tValidateResponse: func(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) error {\n\t\t\t\t\tif resp.Code != 200 {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected status 200, got %d\", resp.Code)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tExpectedError: nil,\n\t\t\tExpectError:   false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tName:        \"request without auth header fails\",\n\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that missing Authorization header returns authentication error\",\n\t\t\tInput: AuthTestInput{\n\t\t\t\tDescription: \"No Authorization header provided\",\n\t\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(req *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\t// No auth header set\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", time.Now().Format(\"20060102T150405Z\"))\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tValidateResponse: func(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) error {\n\t\t\t\t\tif resp.Code != 403 {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected status 403, got %d\", resp.Code)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate S3 error format using testutil helpers\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutil.ValidateErrorCode(resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\"); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error code validation failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate error message contains expected substring\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutil.ValidateErrorMessage(resp, \"Missing Authentication\"); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error message validation failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tExpectedError: ErrMissingAuthHeader,\n\t\t\tExpectError:   true,\n\t\t\t// ErrorContains validates error message contains this substring (case-insensitive)\n\t\t\tErrorContains: \"missing\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tName:        \"request with empty auth header fails\",\n\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that an empty Authorization header returns error\",\n\t\t\tInput: AuthTestInput{\n\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Authorization header is present but empty\",\n\t\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(req *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"\")\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", time.Now().Format(\"20060102T150405Z\"))\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tValidateResponse: func(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) error {\n\t\t\t\t\tif resp.Code != 403 {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected status 403, got %d\", resp.Code)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate that empty auth is treated as missing\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutil.ValidateErrorCode(resp, \"MissingAuthenticationToken\"); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error code validation failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tExpectedError: ErrMissingAuthHeader,\n\t\t\tExpectError:   true,\n\t\t\tErrorContains: \"missing\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\t// RunTable executes all test cases using the provided runner function\n\t// This function handles error checking, test reporting, and validation\n\ttestutil.RunTable(t, table, func(input AuthTestInput) error {\n\t\t// Create mock handler that simulates ARMOR authentication behavior\n\t\thandler := createAuthMockHandler()\n\n\t\t// Build test request using testutil helpers\n\t\treq := testutil.NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\n\t\t// Apply test-specific request configuration\n\t\tif input.SetupRequest != nil {\n\t\t\tinput.SetupRequest(req)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Execute request and capture response\n\t\tresp := testutil.MakeRequest(handler, req)\n\n\t\t// Validate response using test-specific validation\n\t\tif input.ValidateResponse != nil {\n\t\t\treturn input.ValidateResponse(resp)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn nil\n\t})\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION ERROR TEST TABLES - INVALID CREDENTIALS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestAuthError_InvalidCredentials tests requests with invalid credentials.\n//\n// This test demonstrates using the table pattern for testing various invalid\n// credential scenarios including wrong keys and malformed credentials.\nfunc TestAuthError_InvalidCredentials(t *testing.T) {\n\ttable := []testutil.TableTestCase[AuthTestInput, error]{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tName:        \"invalid access key rejected\",\n\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that an unknown access key is rejected\",\n\t\t\tInput: AuthTestInput{\n\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Access key not found in credentials store\",\n\t\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(req *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=INVALIDKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=testsig\")\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", time.Now().Format(\"20060102T150405Z\"))\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tValidateResponse: func(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) error {\n\t\t\t\t\tif resp.Code != 403 {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected status 403, got %d\", resp.Code)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate error code for invalid access key\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutil.ValidateErrorCode(resp, \"InvalidAccessKeyId\"); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error code validation failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate error message\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutil.ValidateErrorMessage(resp, \"does not exist\"); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error message validation failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tExpectedError: ErrInvalidAccessKey,\n\t\t\tExpectError:   true,\n\t\t\tErrorContains: \"access key\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tName:        \"malformed credential string rejected\",\n\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that malformed credential field is rejected\",\n\t\t\tInput: AuthTestInput{\n\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Credential field does not match expected format\",\n\t\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(req *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=malformed, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=testsig\")\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", time.Now().Format(\"20060102T150405Z\"))\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tValidateResponse: func(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) error {\n\t\t\t\t\tif resp.Code != 403 {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected status 403, got %d\", resp.Code)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate error code for malformed credential\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutil.ValidateErrorCode(resp, \"InvalidAccessKeyId\"); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error code validation failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate error message contains \"malformed\"\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutil.ValidateErrorMessage(resp, \"malformed\"); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error message validation failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tExpectedError: ErrInvalidCredential,\n\t\t\tExpectError:   true,\n\t\t\tErrorContains: \"malformed\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tName:        \"signature mismatch rejected\",\n\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that incorrect signature is rejected\",\n\t\t\tInput: AuthTestInput{\n\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Calculated signature does not match provided signature\",\n\t\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(req *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=wrongsignature\")\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", time.Now().Format(\"20060102T150405Z\"))\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tValidateResponse: func(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) error {\n\t\t\t\t\tif resp.Code != 403 {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected status 403, got %d\", resp.Code)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate signature mismatch error code\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutil.ValidateErrorCode(resp, \"SignatureDoesNotMatch\"); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error code validation failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate error message contains \"signature\"\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutil.ValidateErrorMessage(resp, \"signature\"); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error message validation failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tExpectedError: ErrSignatureMismatch,\n\t\t\tExpectError:   true,\n\t\t\tErrorContains: \"signature\",\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\ttestutil.RunTable(t, table, func(input AuthTestInput) error {\n\t\thandler := createAuthMockHandler()\n\t\treq := testutil.NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\n\t\tif input.SetupRequest != nil {\n\t\t\tinput.SetupRequest(req)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresp := testutil.MakeRequest(handler, req)\n\n\t\tif input.ValidateResponse != nil {\n\t\t\treturn input.ValidateResponse(resp)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn nil\n\t})\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// AUTHENTICATION ERROR TEST TABLES - EXPIRED TOKEN\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestAuthError_ExpiredToken tests requests with expired or invalid date headers.\n//\n// This test demonstrates testing temporal validation scenarios using the\n// table-driven pattern, including expired and future-dated requests.\nfunc TestAuthError_ExpiredToken(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Calculate timestamps for temporal testing\n\tnow := time.Now().UTC()\n\texpiredTime := now.Add(-20 * time.Minute) // 20 minutes in the past\n\tfutureTime := now.Add(20 * time.Minute)  // 20 minutes in the future\n\n\ttable := []testutil.TableTestCase[AuthTestInput, error]{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tName:        \"request with expired date header rejected\",\n\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that requests older than 15 minutes are rejected\",\n\t\t\tInput: AuthTestInput{\n\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Request timestamp is outside allowed time window\",\n\t\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(req *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=testsig\")\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", expiredTime.Format(\"20060102T150405Z\"))\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tValidateResponse: func(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) error {\n\t\t\t\t\tif resp.Code != 403 {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected status 403, got %d\", resp.Code)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate expiration error code\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutil.ValidateErrorCode(resp, \"RequestExpired\"); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error code validation failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate error message contains \"expired\"\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutil.ValidateErrorMessage(resp, \"expired\"); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error message validation failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tExpectedError: ErrRequestExpired,\n\t\t\tExpectError:   true,\n\t\t\tErrorContains: \"expired\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tName:        \"request with future date header rejected\",\n\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that future-dated requests are rejected\",\n\t\t\tInput: AuthTestInput{\n\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Request timestamp is in the future\",\n\t\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(req *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=testsig\")\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", futureTime.Format(\"20060102T150405Z\"))\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tValidateResponse: func(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) error {\n\t\t\t\t\tif resp.Code != 403 {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected status 403, got %d\", resp.Code)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate expiration error code (future dates also trigger RequestExpired)\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutil.ValidateErrorCode(resp, \"RequestExpired\"); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error code validation failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate error message contains \"expired\"\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutil.ValidateErrorMessage(resp, \"expired\"); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error message validation failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tExpectedError: ErrRequestExpired,\n\t\t\tExpectError:   true,\n\t\t\tErrorContains: \"expired\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tName:        \"request with missing date header rejected\",\n\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that missing X-Amz-Date header is rejected\",\n\t\t\tInput: AuthTestInput{\n\t\t\t\tDescription: \"X-Amz-Date header is required for authentication\",\n\t\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(req *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=testsig\")\n\t\t\t\t\t// No date header set\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tValidateResponse: func(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) error {\n\t\t\t\t\tif resp.Code != 403 {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected status 403, got %d\", resp.Code)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate missing date header \"date\",\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\t// Success case: valid date header within acceptable window\n\t\t\tName:        \"request with valid date header succeeds\",\n\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that requests within time window are accepted\",\n\t\t\tInput: AuthTestInput{\n\t\t\t\tDescription: \"X-Amz-Date header is within allowed time window\",\n\t\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(req *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=testsig\")\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", now.Format(\"20060102T150405Z\"))\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tValidateResponse: func(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) error {\n\t\t\t\t\t// This should succeed with 200 since all auth parameters are valid\n\t\t\t\t\tif resp.Code != 200 {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"expected status 200, got %d\", resp.Code)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tExpectedError: nil,\n\t\t\tExpectError:   false,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\ttestutil.RunTable(t, table, func(input AuthTestInput) error {\n\t\thandler := createAuthMockHandler()\n\t\treq := testutil.NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\n\t\tif input.SetupRequest != nil {\n\t\t\tinput.SetupRequest(req)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresp := =============================================================================\n// HTTP INTEGRATION TESTS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// TestAuthError_HTTPIntegration tests authentication errors via HTTP responses.\n//\n// This test demonstrates end-to-end testing of authentication errors by\n// simulating HTTP request/response cycles and validating S3 error format.\nfunc TestAuthError_HTTPIntegration(t *testing.T) {\n\ttable := []testutil.TableTestCase[AuthTestInput, error]{\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tName:        \"missing auth returns 403 with S3 error format\",\n\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that missing auth returns proper S3 error response\",\n\t\t\tInput: AuthTestInput{\n\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Validate complete S3 error response structure\",\n\t\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(req *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\t// No auth header\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tValidateResponse: func(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) error {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Use comprehensive assertion helpers from testutil\n\t\t\t\t\ttestutil.AssertAuthenticationError(t, resp)\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tExpectedError: nil,\n\t\t\tExpectError:   false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tName:        \"invalid credentials return 403 with correct error\",\n\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that invalid credentials return proper error response\",\n\t\t\tInput: AuthTestInput{\n\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Validate error response for invalid credentials\",\n\t\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(req *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=INVALID/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=testsig\")\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tValidateResponse: func(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) error {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate status code\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutil.ValidateStatusCode(resp, 403); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"status validation failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate content type\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutil.ValidateContentType(resp, \"application/xml\"); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"content type validation failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t// Validate error code\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutil.ValidateErrorCode(resp, \"InvalidAccessKeyId\"); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"error code validation failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tExpectedError: nil,\n\t\t\tExpectError:   false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t{\n\t\t\tName:        \"valid authentication returns 200\",\n\t\t\tDescription: \"Tests that valid authentication succeeds\",\n\t\t\tInput: AuthTestInput{\n\t\t\t\tDescription: \"Validate successful authentication response\",\n\t\t\t\tSetupRequest: func(req *http.Request) {\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=TESTKEY/20130524/us-east-1/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host, Signature=testsig\")\n\t\t\t\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", time.Now().Format(\"20060102T150405Z\"))\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\tValidateResponse: func(resp *httptest.ResponseRecorder) error {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Valid authentication should return 200\n\t\t\t\t\tif err := testutil.ValidateStatusCode(resp, 200); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"status validation failed: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\tExpectedError: nil,\n\t\t\tExpectError:   false,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\ttestutil.RunTable(t, table, func(input AuthTestInput) error {\n\t\thandler := createAuthMockHandler()\n\t\treq := testutil.NewTestRequest(\"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil)\n\n\t\tif input.SetupRequest != nil {\n\t\t\tinput.SetupRequest(req)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tresp := testutil.MakeRequest(handler, req)\n\n\t\tif input.ValidateResponse != nil {\n\t\t\treturn input.ValidateResponse(resp)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn nil\n\t})\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// MOCK HANDLERS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// createAuthMockHandler creates a test HTTP handler that simulates ARMOR authentication responses.\n//\n// This helper function creates mock HTTP handlers that simulate the ARMOR server's\n// authentication behavior for testing purposes. {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_7a119db58a834af6bdb3b218","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t// Package testutil provides a generic test table framework for organizing test cases.\n2\t//\n3\t// # Generic Test Table Framework\n4\t//\n5\t// This package provides a comprehensive, extensible test table framework for\n6\t// writing table-driven tests in Go. Unlike HTTP-specific test frameworks, this\n7\t// framework is designed to work with any type of test case.\n8\t//\n9\t// # Quick Start\n10\t//\n11\t// Define a test table:\n12\t//\n13\t//\ttable := []TableTestCase[MyInput, MyError]{\n14\t//\t    {\n15\t//\t        Name:        \"valid input succeeds\",\n16\t//\t        Description: \"Tests that valid input produces expected result\",\n17\t//\t        Input:       MyInput{Field: \"value\"},\n18\t//\t        ExpectedError: nil,\n19\t//\t    },\n20\t//\t    {\n21\t//\t        Name:        \"invalid input returns error\",\n22\t//\t        Description: \"Tests that invalid input returns expected error\",\n23\t//\t        Input:       MyInput{Field: \"\"},\n24\t//\t        ExpectedError: ErrInvalidInput,\n25\t//\t    },\n26\t//\t}\n27\t//\n28\t// Run the test table:\n29\t//\n30\t//\tfor _, tc := range table {\n31\t//\t    t.Run(tc.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n32\t//\t        result, err := Process(tc.Input)\n33\t//\t        if tc.ExpectError {\n34\t//\t            if err == nil {\n35\t//\t                t.Errorf(\"expected error, got nil\")\n36\t//\t            }\n37\t//\t        } else {\n38\t//\t            if err != nil {\n39\t//\t                t.Errorf(\"unexpected error: %v\", err)\n40\t//\t            }\n41\t//\t        }\n42\t//\t    })\n43\t//\t}\n44\tpackage testutil\n45\t\n46\timport \"testing\"\n47\t\n48\t// =============================================================================\n49\t// BASE TEST TABLE STRUCTURES\n50\t// =============================================================================\n51\t// These core types form the foundation of the generic test table framework.\n52\t// They are designed to be type-safe, extensible, and work with any error type.\n53\t//\n54\t// Design Philosophy:\n55\t// - Generic: Works with any input and error types\n56\t// - Type-safe: Strong typing prevents errors\n57\t// - Reusable: Structures can be used across test files\n58\t// - Self-documenting: Clear field names and comprehensive documentation\n59\t// =============================================================================\n60\t\n61\t// TableTestCase represents a single generic test case.\n62\t//\n63\t// This is the core building block for generic test tables. Each test case contains\n64\t// all the information needed to execute a single test.\n65\t//\n66\t// Type Parameters:\n67\t//   - I: The input type for the test\n68\t//   - E: The error type (typically error, but can be any custom error type)\n69\t//\n70\t// Use this type when:\n71\t//   - Building generic test tables\n72\t//   - Creating test cases for any function/method\n73\t//   - Writing table-driven tests\n74\t//\n75\t// Example (basic usage):\n76\t//\n77\t//\ttc := TableTestCase[string, error]{\n78\t//\t    Name:        \"empty string is invalid\",\n79\t//\t    Description: \"Tests that empty string returns error\",\n80\t//\t    Input:       \"\",\n81\t//\t    ExpectedError: ErrEmptyString,\n82\t//\t    ExpectError: true,\n83\t//\t}\n84\t//\n85\t// Example (with custom error type):\n86\t//\n87\t//\ttc := TableTestCase[MyInput, MyError]{\n88\t//\t    Name:        \"validation fails\",\n89\t//\t    Description: \"Tests that invalid input returns validation error\",\n90\t//\t    Input:       MyInput{Count: -1},\n91\t//\t    ExpectedError: MyError{Code: ErrValidation},\n92\t//\t    ExpectError: true,\n93\t//\t}\n94\t//\n95\t// Fields:\n96\t//   - Name: Test case name (used in t.Run)\n97\t//   - Description: Optional detailed description of what is being tested\n98\t//   - Input: The input to test\n99\t//   - ExpectedError: The expected error (nil if no error expected)\n100\t//   - ExpectError: Whether an error is expected (convenience field)\n101\t//   - ErrorContains: Optional substring that should be in the error message\n102\t//   - ErrorIs: Optional error that should match using errors.Is\n103\t//   - Skip: Whether to skip this test case\n104\t//   - Tags: Optional tags for test filtering\n105\ttype TableTestCase[I any, E error] struct {\n106\t\t// Name is the test case name for identification and test reporting\n107\t\tName string\n108\t\n109\t\t// Description is an optional detailed explanation of what the test validates\n110\t\tDescription string\n111\t\n112\t\t// Input is the input to test\n113\t\tInput I\n114\t\n115\t\t// ExpectedError is the expected error (nil if no error expected)\n116\t\tExpectedError E\n117\t\n118\t\t// ExpectError indicates whether an error is expected\n119\t\t// This is a convenience field - if true, the test expects an error\n120\t\t// If false, the test expects no error (ExpectedError should be nil)\n121\t\tExpectError bool\n122\t\n123\t\t// ErrorContains is an optional substring that should be in the error message\n124\t\t// If set, the error message must contain this substring\n125\t\tErrorContains string\n126\t\n127\t\t// ErrorIs is an optional error that should match using errors.Is\n128\t\t// If set, errors.Is(actualError, this error) must return true\n129\t\tErrorIs error\n130\t\n131\t\t// Skip optionally marks this test case to be skipped\n132\t\tSkip bool\n133\t\n134\t\t// Tags optionally provides tags for test filtering\n135\t\tTags []string\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t// TableTestTable represents a collection of related generic test cases.\n139\t//\n140\t// This type groups test cases together for organized testing. Tables can be\n141\t// predefined, custom, or extended from existing tables.\n142\t//\n143\t// Type Parameters:\n144\t//   - I: The input type for the test cases\n145\t//   - E: The error type for the test cases\n146\t//\n147\t// Use this type when:\n148\t//   - Creating reusable test tables\n149\t//   - Organizing tests by category\n150\t//   - Sharing test tables across multiple test files\n151\t//\n152\t// Example (creating a table):\n153\t//\n154\t//\ttable := TableTestTable[string, error]{\n155\t//\t    Name:    \"string validation tests\",\n156\t//\t    Description: \"Test cases for string validation\",\n157\t//\t    TestCases: []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n158\t//\t        {Name: \"empty string\", Input: \"\", ExpectedError: ErrEmpty, ExpectError: true},\n159\t//\t        {Name: \"valid string\", Input: \"test\", ExpectedError: nil, ExpectError: false},\n160\t//\t    },\n161\t//\t}\n162\t//\n163\t// Fields:\n164\t//   - Name: The name of the test table\n165\t//   - Description: Optional description of the table's purpose\n166\t//   - TestCases: The collection of test cases\n167\t//   - Tags: Optional tags for the entire table\n168\ttype TableTestTable[I any, E error] struct {\n169\t\t// Name is the name of the test table\n170\t\tName string\n171\t\n172\t\t// Description optionally describes the purpose of this test table\n173\t\tDescription string\n174\t\n175\t\t// TestCases is the collection of test cases\n176\t\tTestCases []TableTestCase[I, E]\n177\t\n178\t\t// Tags optionally provides tags for the entire table\n179\t\tTags []string\n180\t}\n181\t\n182\t// =============================================================================\n183\t// TEST TABLE BUILDER\n184\t// =============================================================================\n185\t// These structures and functions provide a fluent interface for building test tables.\n186\t// =============================================================================\n187\t\n188\t// TableBuilder provides a fluent interface for building test tables.\n189\t//\n190\t// Type Parameters:\n191\t//   - I: The input type for the test cases\n192\t//   - E: The error type for the test cases\n193\t//\n194\t// Use this builder when:\n195\t//   - Creating test tables programmatically\n196\t//   - Building complex test tables step by step\n197\t//   - Adding test cases conditionally\n198\t//\n199\t// Example:\n200\t//\n201\t//\ttable := NewTableBuilder[string, error]().\n202\t//\t    WithName(\"string tests\").\n203\t//\t    WithDescription(\"String validation tests\").\n204\t//\t    WithTestCase(TableTestCase[string, error]{\n205\t//\t        Name: \"empty string\",\n206\t//\t        Input: \"\",\n207\t//\t        ExpectedError: ErrEmpty,\n208\t//\t        ExpectError: true,\n209\t//\t    }).\n210\t//\t    WithTestCase(TableTestCase[string, error]{\n211\t//\t        Name: \"valid string\",\n212\t//\t        Input: \"test\",\n213\t//\t        ExpectedError: nil,\n214\t//\t        ExpectError: false,\n215\t//\t    }).\n216\t//\t    Build()\n217\ttype TableBuilder[I any, E error] struct {\n218\t\ttable TableTestTable[I, E]\n219\t}\n220\t\n221\t// NewTableBuilder creates a new TableBuilder.\n222\t//\n223\t// Example:\n224\t//\n225\t//\tbuilder := NewTableBuilder[string, error]()\n226\tfunc NewTableBuilder[I any, E error]() *TableBuilder[I, E] {\n227\t\treturn &TableBuilder[I, E]{\n228\t\t\ttable: TableTestTable[I, E]{\n229\t\t\t\tTestCases: make([]TableTestCase[I, E], 0),\n230\t\t\t},\n231\t\t}\n232\t}\n233\t\n234\t// WithName sets the name of the test table.\n235\t//\n236\t// Example:\n237\t//\n238\t//\tbuilder := NewTableBuilder[string, error]().\n239\t//\t    WithName(\"my test table\")\n240\tfunc (tb *TableBuilder[I, E]) WithName(name string) *TableBuilder[I, E] {\n241\t\ttb.table.Name = name\n242\t\treturn tb\n243\t}\n244\t\n245\t// WithDescription sets the description of the test table.\n246\t//\n247\t// Example:\n248\t//\n249\t//\tbuilder := NewTableBuilder[string, error]().\n250\t//\t    WithDescription(\"Test cases for string validation\")\n251\tfunc (tb *TableBuilder[I, E]) WithDescription(desc string) *TableBuilder[I, E] {\n252\t\ttb.table.Description = desc\n253\t\treturn tb\n254\t}\n255\t\n256\t// WithTags sets the tags for the test table.\n257\t//\n258\t// Example:\n259\t//\n260\t//\tbuilder := NewTableBuilder[string, error]().\n261\t//\t    WithTags([]string{\"validation\", \"string\"})\n262\tfunc (tb *TableBuilder[I, E]) WithTags(tags []string) *TableBuilder[I, E] {\n263\t\ttb.table.Tags = tags\n264\t\treturn tb\n265\t}\n266\t\n267\t// WithTestCase adds a test case to the table.\n268\t//\n269\t// Example:\n270\t//\n271\t//\tbuilder := NewTableBuilder[string, error]().\n272\t//\t    WithTestCase(TableTestCase[string, error]{\n273\t//\t        Name: \"empty string\",\n274\t//\t        Input: \"\",\n275\t//\t        ExpectedError: ErrEmpty,\n276\t//\t        ExpectError: true,\n277\t//\t    })\n278\tfunc (tb *TableBuilder[I, E]) WithTestCase(tc TableTestCase[I, E]) *TableBuilder[I, E] {\n279\t\ttb.table.TestCases = append(tb.table.TestCases, tc)\n280\t\treturn tb\n281\t}\n282\t\n283\t// WithTestCases adds multiple test cases to the table.\n284\t//\n285\t// Example:\n286\t//\n287\t//\tcases := []TableTestCase[string, error]{...}\n288\t//\tbuilder := NewTableBuilder[string, error]().\n289\t//\t    WithTestCases(cases)\n290\tfunc (tb *TableBuilder[I, E]) WithTestCases(cases []TableTestCase[I, E]) *TableBuilder[I, E] {\n291\t\ttb.table.TestCases = append(tb.table.TestCases, cases...)\n292\t\treturn tb\n293\t}\n294\t\n295\t// Build constructs and returns the test table.\n296\t//\n297\t// Example:\n298\t//\n299\t//\ttable := NewTableBuilder[string, error]().\n300\t//\t    WithName(\"string tests\").\n301\t//\t    WithTestCase(tc1).\n302\t//\t    WithTestCase(tc2).\n303\t//\t    Build()\n304\tfunc (tb *TableBuilder[I, E]) Build() TableTestTable[I, E] {\n305\t\treturn tb.table\n306\t}\n307\t\n308\t// =============================================================================\n309\t// RUNNER FUNCTIONS\n310\t// =============================================================================\n311\t// These functions provide convenient ways to run test tables.\n312\t// =============================================================================\n313\t\n314\t// RunTableFunc is the function signature for running a single test case.\n315\t//\n316\t// Type Parameters:\n317\t//   - I: The input type for the test case\n318\t//\n319\t// Parameters:\n320\t//   - input: The input to test\n321\t//\n322\t// Returns:\n323\t//   - error: The error from the test (if any)\n324\t//\n325\t// Example implementation:\n326\t//\n327\t//\tfunc myValidator(input string) error {\n328\t//\t    if input == \"\" {\n329\t//\t        return ErrEmpty\n330\t//\t    }\n331\t//\t    return nil\n332\t//\t}\n333\t//\n334\t//\tvar runner RunTableFunc[string] = myValidator\n335\ttype RunTableFunc[I any] func(input I) error\n336\t\n337\t// RunTable executes all test cases in a table using the provided runner function.\n338\t//\n339\t// This function iterates through all test cases in the table and executes them\n340\t// using the provided runner function. It handles error checking, skipping,\n341\t// and test reporting.\n342\t//\n343\t// Type Parameters:\n344\t//   - I: The input type for the test cases\n345\t//\n346\t// Parameters:\n347\t//   - t: The testing.T instance for test reporting\n348\t//   - table: The test table to run\n349\t//   - runner: The function to execute for each test case\n350\t//\n351\t// Example:\n352\t//\n353\t//\tfunc TestStringValidation(t *testing.T) {\n354\t//\t    table := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n355\t//\t        {\n356\t//\t            Name:        \"empty string returns error\",\n357\t//\t            Input:       \"\",\n358\t//\t            ExpectedError: ErrEmpty,\n359\t//\t            ExpectError: true,\n360\t//\t        },\n361\t//\t        {\n362\t//\t            Name:        \"valid string succeeds\",\n363\t//\t            Input:       \"test\",\n364\t//\t            ExpectedError: nil,\n365\t//\t            ExpectError: false,\n366\t//\t        },\n367\t//\t    }\n368\t//\n369\t//\t    RunTable(t, table, func(input string) error {\n370\t//\t        return validateString(input)\n371\t//\t    })\n372\t//\t}\n373\tfunc RunTable[I any](t interface {\n374\t\tHelper()\n375\t\tRun(name string, f func(t *testing.T)) bool\n376\t\tSkipf(format string, args ...interface{})\n377\t\tErrorf(format string, args ...interface{})\n378\t}, table []TableTestCase[I, error], runner RunTableFunc[I]) {\n379\t\tt.Helper()\n380\t\n381\t\tfor _, tc := range table {\n382\t\t\tt.Run(tc.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n383\t\t\t\tt.Helper()\n384\t\n385\t\t\t\tif tc.Skip {\n386\t\t\t\t\tt.Skipf(\"test case skipped: %s\", tc.Name)\n387\t\t\t\t\treturn\n388\t\t\t\t}\n389\t\n390\t\t\t\terr := runner(tc.Input)\n391\t\n392\t\t\t\t// Check if error is expected\n393\t\t\t\tif tc.ExpectError {\n394\t\t\t\t\tif err == nil {\n395\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: expected error, got nil\", tc.Name)\n396\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n397\t\t\t\t\t}\n398\t\n399\t\t\t\t\t// Check ErrorContains\n400\t\t\t\t\tif tc.ErrorContains != \"\" {\n401\t\t\t\t\t\terrMsg := err.Error()\n402\t\t\t\t\t\tif !contains(errMsg, tc.ErrorContains) {\n403\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: error message does not contain expected substring\\n\"+\n404\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"expected substring: %s\\nactual error: %v\", tc.Name, tc.ErrorContains, err)\n405\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n406\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n407\t\t\t\t\t}\n408\t\n409\t\t\t\t\t// Check ErrorIs\n410\t\t\t\t\tif tc.ErrorIs != nil {\n411\t\t\t\t\t\tif !errorIs(err, tc.ErrorIs) {\n412\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: error does not match expected error\\n\"+\n413\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"expected: %v\\nactual: %v\", tc.Name, tc.ErrorIs, err)\n414\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n415\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n416\t\t\t\t\t}\n417\t\n418\t\t\t\t\t// Check ExpectedError\n419\t\t\t\t\tif tc.ExpectedError != nil {\n420\t\t\t\t\t\tif !errorIs(err, tc.ExpectedError) {\n421\t\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: error does not match expected error\\n\"+\n422\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\"expected: %v\\nactual: %v\", tc.Name, tc.ExpectedError, err)\n423\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n424\t\t\t\t\t}\n425\t\t\t\t} else {\n426\t\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n427\t\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: unexpected error: %v\", tc.Name, err)\n428\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n429\t\t\t\t\t}\n430\t\t\t\t}\n431\t\t\t})\n432\t\t}\n433\t}\n434\t\n435\t// contains is a helper to check if a string contains a substring.\n436\tfunc contains(s, substr string) bool {\n437\t\treturn len(s) >= len(substr) && (s == substr ||\n438\t\t\tlen(s) > len(substr) && findSubstring(s, substr))\n439\t}\n440\t\n441\t// findSubstring is a simple substring search.\n442\tfunc findSubstring(s, substr string) bool {\n443\t\tfor i := 0; i <= len(s)-len(substr); i++ {\n444\t\t\tif s[i:i+len(substr)] == substr {\n445\t\t\t\treturn true\n446\t\t\t}\n447\t\t}\n448\t\treturn false\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t// errorIs is a helper to check if an error matches another error.\n452\tfunc errorIs(err, target error) bool {\n453\t\tif err == nil && target == nil {\n454\t\t\treturn true\n455\t\t}\n456\t\tif err == nil || target == nil {\n457\t\t\treturn false\n458\t\t}\n459\t\treturn err.Error() == target.Error() || contains(err.Error(), target.Error())\n460\t}\n461\t\n462\t// =============================================================================\n463\t// TABLE EXTENSION HELPERS\n464\t// =============================================================================\n465\t// These functions provide patterns for extending and manipulating test tables.\n466\t// =============================================================================\n467\t\n468\t// ExtendTable adds custom test cases to an existing test table.\n469\t//\n470\t// This helper provides a clean pattern for extending predefined test tables\n471\t// with custom test cases without modifying the original table.\n472\t//\n473\t// Type Parameters:\n474\t//   - I: The input type for the test cases\n475\t//   - E: The error type for the test cases\n476\t//\n477\t// Parameters:\n478\t//   - base: The base test table to extend\n479\t//   - customCases: Custom test cases to add\n480\t//\n481\t// Returns a new test table containing both base and custom cases\n482\t//\n483\t// Example:\n484\t//\n485\t//\tbase := []TableTestCase[string, error]{...}\n486\t//\tcustom := []TableTestCase[string, error]{\n487\t//\t    {Name: \"custom test\", Input: \"custom\", ExpectedError: nil, ExpectError: false},\n488\t//\t}\n489\t//\textended := ExtendTable(base, custom)\n490\tfunc ExtendTable[I any, E error](base []TableTestCase[I, E], customCases []TableTestCase[I, E]) []TableTestCase[I, E] {\n491\t\textended := make([]TableTestCase[I, E], len(base)+len(customCases))\n492\t\tcopy(extended, base)\n493\t\tcopy(extended[len(base):], customCases)\n494\t\treturn extended\n495\t}\n496\t\n497\t// FilterTable filters a test table by tag.\n498\t//\n499\t// This helper allows selective test execution based on tags.\n500\t// Useful for running specific subsets of tests.\n501\t//\n502\t// Type Parameters:\n503\t//   - I: The input type for the test cases\n504\t//   - E: The error type for the test cases\n505\t//\n506\t// Parameters:\n507\t//   - table: The test table to filter\n508\t//   - tag: The tag to filter by\n509\t//\n510\t// Returns a filtered test table\n511\t//\n512\t// Example:\n513\t//\n514\t//\tallTests := []TableTestCase[string, error]{...}\n515\t//\tskipTests := FilterTable(allTests, \"skip\")\n516\tfunc FilterTable[I any, E error](table []TableTestCase[I, E], tag string) []TableTestCase[I, E] {\n517\t\tvar filtered []TableTestCase[I, E]\n518\t\tfor _, tc := range table {\n519\t\t\tfor _, t := range tc.Tags {\n520\t\t\t\tif t == tag {\n521\t\t\t\t\tfiltered = append(filtered, tc)\n522\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n523\t\t\t\t}\n524\t\t\t}\n525\t\t}\n526\t\treturn filtered\n527\t}\n528\t\n529\t// FilterTableByTag filters a test table to exclude specific tags.\n530\t//\n531\t// This helper allows selective test execution by excluding tests with specific tags.\n532\t// Useful for excluding tests marked with specific tags (e.g., \"skip\", \"slow\").\n533\t//\n534\t// Type Parameters:\n535\t//   - I: The input type for the test cases\n536\t//   - E: The error type for the test cases\n537\t//\n538\t// Parameters:\n539\t//   - table: The test table to filter\n540\t//   - excludeTag: The tag to exclude\n541\t//\n542\t// Returns a filtered test table excluding tests with the specified tag\n543\t//\n544\t// Example:\n545\t//\n546\t//\tallTests := []TableTestCase[string, error]{...}\n547\t//\twithoutSkip := FilterTableByTag(allTests, \"skip\")\n548\tfunc FilterTableByTag[I any, E error](table []TableTestCase[I, E], excludeTag string) []TableTestCase[I, E] {\n549\t\tvar filtered []TableTestCase[I, E]\n550\t\tfor _, tc := range table {\n551\t\t\thasExcludeTag := false\n552\t\t\tfor _, t := range tc.Tags {\n553\t\t\t\tif t == excludeTag {\n554\t\t\t\t\thasExcludeTag = true\n555\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n556\t\t\t\t}\n557\t\t\t}\n558\t\t\tif !hasExcludeTag {\n559\t\t\t\tfiltered = append(filtered, tc)\n560\t\t\t}\n561\t\t}\n562\t\treturn filtered\n563\t}\n564\t\n565\t// nil,\n592\t\t\tExpectError:  false,\n593\t\t}\n594\t}\n595\t\n596\t// ErrorCase creates a test case for error execution.\n597\t//\n598\t// This helper creates a test case that expects an     name,\n616\t\t\tInput:         input,\n617\t\t\tExpectedError: expectedErr,\n618\t\t\tExpectError:   true,\n619\t\t}\n620\t}\n621\t\n622\t// ErrorCaseWithMessage creates a test case for error true,\n646\t\t\tErrorContains: errorMsg,\n647\t\t}\n648\t}\n649\t\n650\t// =============================================================================\n651\t// TEST EXECUTION HELPERS\n652\t// =============================================================================\n655\t\n656\t// AssertNoError asserts that no error occurred.\n657\t//\n658\t// This helper fails the test if an error is present.\n659\t//\n660\t// Parameters:\n661\t//   - t: The testing.T instance\n662\t//   - err: The error to check\n663\t//   - msg: Optional message prefix\n664\t//\n665\t// Example:\n666\t//\n667\t//\tAssertNoError(t, err, \"validation failed\")\n668\tfunc AssertNoError(t *testing.T, err error, msg ...string) {\n669\t\tt.Helper()\n670\t\tif err != nil {\n671\t\t\tprefix := \"\"\n672\t\t\tif len(msg) > 0 {\n673\t\t\t\tprefix = msg[0] + \": \"\n674\t\t\t}\n675\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%sunexpected error: %v\", prefix, err)\n676\t\t}\n677\t}\n678\t\n679\t// AssertError asserts that an error occurred.\n680\t//\n681\t// This helper fails the test if no error is present.\n682\t//\n683\t// Parameters:\n684\t//   - t: The testing.T instance\n685\t//   - err: The error to check\n686\t//   - msg: Optional message prefix\n687\t//\n688\t// Example:\n689\t//\n690\t//\tAssertError(t, err, \"expected validation to fail\")\n691\tfunc AssertError(t *testing.T, err error, msg ...string) {\n692\t\tt.Helper()\n693\t\tif err == nil {\n694\t\t\tprefix := \"\"\n695\t\t\tif len(msg) > 0 {\n696\t\t\t\tprefix = msg[0] + \": \"\n697\t\t\t}\n698\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%sexpected error, got nil\", prefix)\n699\t\t}\n700\t}\n701\t\n702\t// AssertErrorIs asserts that the error matches the expected error.\n703\t//\n704\t// This helper fails the test if the error does not match the expected error.\n705\t//\n706\t// Parameters:\n707\t//   - t: The testing.T instance\n708\t//   - err: The error to check\n709\t//   - expected: The expected error\n710\t//   - msg: Optional message prefix\n711\t//\n712\t// Example:\n713\t//\n714\t//\tAssertErrorIs(t, err, ErrInvalidInput, \"error type mismatch\")\n715\tfunc AssertErrorIs(t *testing.T, err error, expected error, msg ...string) {\n716\t\tt.Helper()\n717\t\tif err == nil {\n718\t\t\tprefix := \"\"\n719\t\t\tif len(msg) > 0 {\n720\t\t\t\tprefix = msg[0] + \": \"\n721\t\t\t}\n722\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%sexpected error %v, got nil\", prefix, expected)\n723\t\t\treturn\n724\t\t}\n725\t\tif !errorIs(err, expected) {\n726\t\t\tprefix := \"\"\n727\t\t\tif len(msg) > 0 {\n728\t\t\t\tprefix = msg[0] + \": \"\n729\t\t\t}\n730\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%sexpected error %v, got %v\", prefix, expected, err)\n731\t\t}\n732\t}\n733\t\n734\t// AssertErrorContains asserts that the error message contains a substring.\n735\t//\n736\t// This helper fails the test if the error message does not contain the substring.\n737\t//\n738\t// Parameters:\n739\t//   - t: The testing.T instance\n740\t//   - err: The error to check\n741\t//   - substring: The substring to look for\n742\t//   - msg: Optional message prefix\n743\t//\n744\t// Example:\n745\t//\n746\t//\tAssertErrorContains(t, err, \"invalid input\", \"error message mismatch\")\n747\tfunc AssertErrorContains(t *testing.T, err error, substring string, msg ...string) {\n748\t\tt.Helper()\n749\t\tif err == nil {\n750\t\t\tprefix := \"\"\n751\t\t\tif len(msg) > 0 {\n752\t\t\t\tprefix = msg[0] + \": \"\n753\t\t\t}\n754\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%serror message should contain %q, got nil\", prefix, substring)\n755\t\t\treturn\n756\t\t}\n757\t\tif
